We’re a community with over 2,500 colleagues all over the world, working to serve customers, and partners in over 190 countries and territories.By taking the complexity out of the financial workflows–including everything from global payments and compliance to multi-currency and w
_full_description: About PayoneerFounded in 2005, Payoneer is the global financial platform that removes friction from doing business across borders, with a mission to connect the world’s underserved businesses to a rising global economy. We’re a community with over 2,500 colleagues all over the world, working to serve customers, and partners in over 190 countries and territories.By taking the complexity out of the financial workflows–including everything from global payments and compliance to multi-currency and workforce management, to providing working capital and business intelligence–we give businesses the tools they need to work efficiently worldwide and grow with confidence.About Payoneer Founded in 2005, Payoneer is the global financial platform that removes friction from doing business across borders, with a mission to connect the world’s underserved businesses to a rising global economy. We’re a community with over 2,500 colleagues all over the world, working to serve customers, and partners in over 190 markets.By taking the complexity out of the financial workflows–including everything from global payments and compliance to multi-currency and workforce management, to providing working capital and business intelligence–we give businesses the tools they need to work efficiently worldwide and grow with confidence.Role Summary We’re looking for a Director of Data who is a tech-enthusiast with a drive for excellence, and an out-of-the-box mindset to satisfy business needs in a complex payment solution environment.Lead Payoneer’s Data function in India as the primary POC for the global Data organization, covering BI/DE/DPM/Analytics. Own all sprint work, ensuring India’s full workplan is represented in planning ceremonies and executed with high quality.Location - Gurugram (Work from Office) What You'll Do: Leadership & Site Ownership – Manage and develop the India Data team while representing global data disciplines locally; ensure clear standards, strong execution, and alignment. Sprint Ownership & Workplan Execution – Own the full sprint cycle end-to-end; represent India in planning, highlight dependencies, and ensure committed work is delivered. Orchestration Across Data Functions – Coordinate ingestion, modeling, reporting, data product work, and governance; remove blockers and ensure seamless collaboration with global teams. Data Product & Requirements Management – Translate business needs into scoped data requirements; ensure consistent definitions, modeling standards, and high-quality outputs. Governance, Quality & Compliance – Enforce data governance, documentation, PII and audit processes; ensure India deliverables meet data quality and compliance expectations. Modernization & Platform Alignment – Lead India execution for cloud migration, Looker rollout, semantic layer, and event framework adoption; drive adherence to modern best practices. Cross-Functional Collaboration – Partner with product, engineering, compliance, and domain leaders; communicate progress, risks, and capacity clearly across global forums. What We're Looking For: 8–12+ years in Data/Analytics with 4+ years in leadership roles. Strong background in BI, Data Engineering, or Data Product Management. Experience leading cross-functional data teams in global/matrix setups. Hands-on knowledge of modern data stacks (BigQuery, Looker). Excellent communication, prioritization, and stakeholder-management skills. Ability to drive structure, alignment, and predictable delivery. Nice to have Experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP). Experience with AI tools. Proficiency in data modeling techniques. Knowledge and understanding of the payments industry. Bachelor's degree in industrial engineering/information systems or equivalent. What Success Looks Like India becomes a predictable, autonomous, high-quality extension of the global Data org. Sprint commitments are consistently delivered with clear visibility and alignment. Stakeholders experience smooth collaboration and strong delivery quality from India. Governance and audit standards are met consistently with minimal rework. India accelerates modernization initiatives (Looker, BigQuery, event frameworks). The Payoneer Ways of WorkingAct as our customer’s partner on the insideLearning what they need and creating what will help them go further.Do it. Own it.Being fearlessly accountable in everything we do.Continuously improveAlways striving for a higher standard than our last.Build Each Other UpHelping each other grow, as professionals and people.If this sounds like a business, a community, and a mission you want to be part of, apply today.We are committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace. Payoneer is an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment no matter your race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any stage of the hiring process, please speak to the recruiter managing the role for any adjustments. Decisions about requests for reasonable accommodation are made on a case-by-case basis.

title: Associate Director, Customer Success, MidMarket Americas
description_excerpt: About The RolePubMatic is hiring an Associate Director, Customer Success, to join our growing team in Gurugram. The Customer Success (CS) team are the primary owners of PubMatic’s global publisher base. As an Associate Director, CS in the MidMarket org, you will oversee a team of Team Leads and Customer Success Operations Managers (CSOMs) supporting MidMarket accounts for the US region.We are looking for a highly motivated and experienced team contributor to manage and help build a team that sup
_full_description: About The RolePubMatic is hiring an Associate Director, Customer Success, to join our growing team in Gurugram. The Customer Success (CS) team are the primary owners of PubMatic’s global publisher base. As an Associate Director, CS in the MidMarket org, you will oversee a team of Team Leads and Customer Success Operations Managers (CSOMs) supporting MidMarket accounts for the US region.We are looking for a highly motivated and experienced team contributor to manage and help build a team that supports the US medium to long-tail publisher business. You will support your team as they manage omnichannel supply (desktop, video, mobile, CTV), foster and develop partnerships with publishers, optimize account performance, run data analysis, and technical troubleshooting.To achieve this, you will need to understand our core business strategy and be able to execute the tactics required, as well as having a deep understanding of PubMatic’s product offerings and market forces to guide and support your team.This role reports into the Senior Director, Customer Success.What You'll DoManage a large team of direct reports, including their ongoing development, as well as recruit, hire, and train talent to support continued team growth.Oversee relationship management of a large book of publisher accounts, ensuring excellent service, aiding in relationship growth and hitting/exceeding revenue targets through optimizations and cross-sales/upsells.Ensure operational excellence as primary technical escalation point for team, including new publisher onboarding of header bidding integrations, performance optimization, and technical troubleshooting.Must be a team player that can manage team technical escalations via Salesforce, Jira and other platforms. Additionally, develop a fantastic team environment within and across the teams to work cohesively.Coach peers proactively in the organization to create skill lift that drives client growth and contributes to overall team success.Develop a culture of collaboration and accountability across the team.Collaborate with Publisher Development team and Product Commercial Leads to find additional opportunities and assist in upselling.Work closely with the Advertiser Solutions and Tech Ops teams to create win-win solutions for both the demand and supply side.Oversee Salesforce hygiene and reporting of team’s publisher accounts, customer data, Opportunity pipeline, Case workflow, and publisher performance & revenue data.Identify product enhancement opportunities and liaison with Product Managers to strengthen and innovate PubMatic’s Product offering.We'd Love for You to HavePrevious management experience required (manager, lead or supervisor role).Experience leading a large team preferred.Minimum 8 years of relevant programmatic ad tech work experience in the digital advertising industry, including in-depth knowledge of areas such as Google AdMob, Google Ad Manager, SSP & DSP ecosystem, Prebid, and other header bidding wrappers.Preferred knowledge in areas such as video (in-steam, outstream, CTV & OTT), mobile app/SDK, ad quality/brand safety, inventory quality, PMP, oRTB, ad tags.Knowledge to use debugging tools like Charles, Fiddler, and Firebug, etc.Experience supporting a global customer base and interfacing with a US-based team.Demonstrated experience in publisher relationship management, including excellent service, relationship growth, and cross-sales/upsells.Proactive, quick learner, detail-oriented, strong problem-solving skills.Advanced publisher performance optimization and reporting skills.Very strong analytical thinking abilities, project management, and organization skills.Ability to present clear project plans and communicate with teams to reach desired results.Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) preferred.Ability to work flexible hours (generally local business hours except for meetings that overlap with US (California) mornings).QualificationBCA/B.Tech/B.E./MCA graduation degree or equivalent from a well-known institute/university.Additional InformationReturn to Office: PubMatic employees throughout the global have returned to our offices via a hybrid work schedule (3 days “in office” and 2 days “working remotely”) that is intended to maximize collaboration, innovation, and productivity among teams and across functions.Benefits: Our benefits package includes the best of what leading organizations provide, such as paternity/maternity leave, healthcare insurance, and broadband reimbursement. As well, when we’re back in the office, we all benefit from a kitchen loaded with healthy snacks and drinks and catered lunches, and much more!Diversity and Inclusion: PubMatic is proud to be an equal opportunity employer; we don’t just value diversity, we promote and celebrate it. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.About PubMaticPubMatic is one of the world’s leading scaled digital advertising platforms, offering more transparent advertising solutions to publishers, media buyers, commerce companies, and data owners, allowing them to harness the power and potential of the open internet to drive better business outcomes.Founded in 2006 with the vision that data-driven decisioning would be the future of digital advertising, we enable content creators to run a more profitable advertising business, which in turn allows them to invest back into the multi-screen and multi-format content that consumers demand.

title: Director, Deal Strategy
description_excerpt: We are a global team of innovators and pioneers dedicated to shaping the future of observability. At New Relic, we build an intelligent platform that empowers companies to thrive in an AI-first world by giving them unparalleled insight into their complex systems. As we continue to expand our global footprint, we're looking for passionate people to join our mission. If you're ready to help the world's best companies optimize their digital applications, we invite you to explore a career with us!Di
_full_description: We are a global team of innovators and pioneers dedicated to shaping the future of observability. At New Relic, we build an intelligent platform that empowers companies to thrive in an AI-first world by giving them unparalleled insight into their complex systems. As we continue to expand our global footprint, we're looking for passionate people to join our mission. If you're ready to help the world's best companies optimize their digital applications, we invite you to explore a career with us!Director - Deal Management EMEAOpportunityNew Relic is seeking a proven and experienced leader to manage and scale our EMEA Deal Management Team. As part of this role, you will exercise strong business acumen in making decisions on go/no-go opinions for the company’s most strategic & complex deals. This includes the creation and alignment of commercial frameworks, custom pricing, and leading of pricing improvement initiatives. In parallel, you will be using pricing analytics for decision support and providing feedback to the internal Deal Management team along with other business partners. Your work will drive executive decisions that are core to our long-term success!This role leads New Relic’s EMEA Deal Management team working with Sales teams to vet and formulate repeatable commercial frameworks to help New Relic negotiate more effective agreements for our important accounts. This role will also have the chance to lead the deal execution process, which requires collaborating with multiple functions such as Sales, Revenue Recognition, Finance, Legal, Product Management and Sales Ops to provide governance for non-standard deal approvals and supporting contractual terms. Helping enable these teams as new pricing and packaging strategies develop will be part of the sales collaboration.ResponsibilitiesLead the EMEA Deal Management team, operating framework, end-to-end process, systems, & metrics for deal management functionOversee commercial terms and non-standard approvalsAccountable for deal structuring practices & policies that accelerate growth, improve sales velocity and ensure fair value for New Relic and customersActs as senior escalation point and approval authority in commercial negotiationsLead projects and initiatives focused on efficiency and simplifying processes throughout the sales organizationEnhance, track and report deal metrics and make recommendations to improve functional & financial performancePartner with Sales, Product Management, Legal and Finance to develop programs to enhance deal profitability and ensure New Relic’s interests are protectedPerform regular data analysis to observe and drive enhanced productivity across deal development and deal cycle times.Operate at the highest levels of integrity and transparency and appropriately address any gaps in these areasProvide financial modeling and analysis as needed to support pricing, discount, and go-to-market investment decisionsQualifications10+ years of Operations/Sales Operations/Finance management experienceBachelor’s in finance, accounting or adjacent fieldDemonstrated understanding of Consumption and/or SaaS go-to-market modelsProven success leading & scaling multi-discipline Finance or Operations teamsHighly analytical, fact driven, process orientated, but comfortable challenging the status quo and setting new strategic/operational directionExcellent verbal, written, and presentation communication skillsCombination of strong intellect, creative problem solving, effective communication with colleagues and willingness to roll up sleeves to improve performanceStrong business acumen, ideally in a software related environmentA consistent track record of using deep analytical skills to see beyond the numbers to drive strategyBonus points if you haveExperience managing the Sales cycle and structuring SaaS deals, especially those of significant valueSFDC and ERP systems experience a plusFostering a diverse, welcoming and inclusive environment is important to us. We work hard to make everyone feel comfortable bringing their best, most authentic selves to work every day. We celebrate our talented Relics’ different backgrounds and abilities, and recognize the different paths they took to reach us – including nontraditional ones. Their experiences and perspectives inspire us to make our products and company the best they can be. We’re looking for people who feel connected to our mission and values, not just candidates who check off all the boxes.If you require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application or recruiting process, please reach out to resume@newrelic.com.We believe in empowering all Relics to achieve professional and business success through a flexible workforce model. This model allows us to work in a variety of workplaces that best support our success, including fully office-based, fully remote, or hybrid.Our hiring processIn compliance with applicable law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work and to complete employment eligibility verification. Note: Our stewardship of the data of thousands of customers means that a criminal background check is required to join New Relic.We will consider qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records based on individual circumstances and in accordance with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. New Relic does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes, and will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with New Relic.New Relic develops and distributes encryption software and technology that complies with U.S. export controls and licensing requirements. Certain New Relic roles require candidates to pass an export compliance assessment as a condition of employment in any global location. If relevant, we will provide more information later in the application process.Candidates are evaluated based on qualifications, regardless of race, religion, ethnicity, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, age, disability, neurodiversity, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other legally protected characteristics.Review our Applicant Privacy Notice at https://newrelic.com/termsandconditions/applicant-privacy-policy

title: Treasury Manager - New Products
description_excerpt: About TideAt Tide we help SMEs save time (and money) in the running of their businesses by not only offering business accounts and related banking services, but also a comprehensive set of highly usable and connected administrative solutions from invoicing to accounting.Tide is transforming the small business banking market with over 1.8 million members globally across the UK, India, Germany and France.Using advanced technology, all solutions are designed with SMEs in mind. With quick onboarding
_full_description: About TideAt Tide we help SMEs save time (and money) in the running of their businesses by not only offering business accounts and related banking services, but also a comprehensive set of highly usable and connected administrative solutions from invoicing to accounting.Tide is transforming the small business banking market with over 1.8 million members globally across the UK, India, Germany and France.Using advanced technology, all solutions are designed with SMEs in mind. With quick onboarding, low fees and innovative features, we thrive on making data-driven decisions to serve our mission: to help SMEs save both time (and money) so they can get back to doing what they love.Tide FactsTide is available for UK, Indian, German and French SMEsOver 1.8 million members: 800,000 UK and 1,000,000 in India and growing rapidlyOver $300 million raised in fundingOver 2,500 Tideans globally - we’re diversity champions!We have offices in Central London, with a member support and technology centre in Sofia, Bulgaria, technology centres in Serbia, Romania, Lithuania and Hyderabad and offices in Gurugram and New Delhi, and in Berlin, Paris and Luxembourg.About The TeamThe Treasury team acts as 1st LoD and is at the forefront of driving financial excellence and supporting strategic growth. The Treasury team mission is to optimise the company's financial resources and investment, manage financial risks, ensure robust liquidity and effective cash management and comprehensive treasury operations.Moreover the team collaborates closely with the product and business team to align financial strategies with business objectives. By contributing to impactful financial decisions, the Treasury team aims to enable sustainable success and growth.About The RoleAs a Treasury Manager – New Products, you will lead the end-to-end implementation of new product initiatives from a Treasury perspective. Acting as the primary Treasury representative, you will drive the design, build, and operational readiness of new product flows and market launches.This is a high-impact, high-visibility role, where you will work closely with Product, Engineering, Payments, and senior leadership to ensure Treasury requirements are embedded across the full product lifecycle.Product Delivery & Stakeholder ManagementAct as the primary Treasury point of contact for Product and Engineering teams.Own front-to-back delivery of new product initiatives, including design, testing, implementation, and go-live.Translate Treasury requirements into product and technical roadmaps.Drive cross-functional collaboration across Treasury, Payments, Compliance, and Technology teams.Manage project plans, timelines, risks, and dependencies across multiple workstreams.Engage, influence, and where required push back on senior stakeholders to ensure Treasury priorities are met.Treasury Transformation & AutomationIdentify and deliver automation opportunities across treasury processes (cash management, payments, forecasting, bank reporting).Lead process re-engineering to support scalable and standardised new product flows.Partner with Product and Engineering to implement efficient, future-ready treasury infrastructure.Data, Reporting & ControlsImprove data quality, reporting, and transparency across treasury activities.Build and enhance dashboards and MIS for liquidity, payments, and forecasting.Ensure all implementations align with treasury strategy, safeguarding requirements, and internal controls.Support Treasury readiness for new markets, products, and regulatory requirements.What We Are Looking ForGood relevant years of experience in Treasury, Payments, or Financial Services transformation roles.Proven track record of delivering end-to-end product or system implementations.Strong understanding of treasury operations, payments ecosystems, and safeguarding.Experience in fintech or high-growth environments preferred.Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with ability to influence senior leaders.Self-starter with high ownership, comfortable working in ambiguity and fast-paced environments.Experience with BI/reporting tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) is a plus.What You’ll Get In ReturnCompetitive Compensation - competitive salary and share optionsTime Off – Generous annual leave on top of bank holidays.Parental Leave – Paid maternity, paternity, and adoption leave to support your family journey.Sabbatical – Extended unpaid and paid leave options after completing milestone years with Tide.Health Insurance – Private family insurance with additional OPD coverage and top-up options.Life & Accident Cover – Comprehensive accidental and life insurance protection.Mental Wellbeing – Access to therapy sessions, courses, meditations, and workshops.Volunteering & Development Days – Paid days annually for volunteering or personal growth.Learning & Development – Annual budget for books, courses, coaching, and more.WOO (Work Outside the Office) – Work from abroad for up to 90 days annually.Home Office Setup – Contribution towards setting up your home officeLaptop Ownership – Keep your old laptop and get a new one when it’s time for a replacement.Snacks & Meals – Office perks with snacks, coffee, tea, and lunch (location dependent).TIDE IS A PLACE FOR EVERYONEAt Tide, we believe that we can only succeed if we let our differences enrich our culture. Our Tideans come from a variety of backgrounds and experience levels. We consider everyone irrespective of their ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity or differently-abled status. We celebrate diversity in our workforce as a cornerstone of our success. Our commitment to a broad spectrum of ideas and backgrounds is what enables us to build products that resonate with our members’ diverse needs and lives.We are One Team and foster a transparent and inclusive environment, where everyone’s voice is heard.At Tide, we thrive on diversity, embracing various backgrounds and experiences. We welcome all individuals regardless of ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability. Our inclusive culture is key to our success, helping us build products that meet our members' diverse needs. We are One Team, committed to transparency and ensuring everyone’s voice is heard.DisclaimerIt Has Come To Our Attention That Individuals Or Agencies Are Falsely Claiming To Represent Tide And Are Reaching Out To Candidates Regarding Job Opportunities. Please Be Aware ThatTide does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process.All official Tide job opportunities are listed exclusively on our Careers Page and applications should be submitted through this channel.Communication from Tide will only come from an official @tide.co email address.Tide does not work with agencies or recruiters without prior formal engagement, and we do not authorize third parties to make job offers on our behalf.If you are contacted by anyone misrepresenting Tide or requesting payment, please treat it as fraudulent and report it to us immediately at talent@tide.coYour safety and trust are important to us, and we are committed to ensuring a fair and transparent recruitment process.Tide leverages AI to enhance our hiring experience. You can read more about how we use AI in our recruitment process in our AI Policy.Your personal data will be processed by Tide for recruitment purposes and in accordance with Tide's Recruitment Privacy Notice.

title: Market Unit Manager, FNN
description_excerpt: Job DetailsJob Title: Market Unit Manager (Food & Nutrition)Location: Mumbai, IndiaWork Arrangement: HybridAbout The RoleWe are looking for an experienced and motivated Market Unit Manager in Food and Nutrition to lead a high-performance sales team and drive the business unit’s growth by aggressively acquiring new customers and expanding revenue from existing accounts. This role demands a strong sales focus, leadership skills, and the ability to inspire a team to achieve ambitious sales targets.
_full_description: Job DetailsJob Title: Market Unit Manager (Food & Nutrition)Location: Mumbai, IndiaWork Arrangement: HybridAbout The RoleWe are looking for an experienced and motivated Market Unit Manager in Food and Nutrition to lead a high-performance sales team and drive the business unit’s growth by aggressively acquiring new customers and expanding revenue from existing accounts. This role demands a strong sales focus, leadership skills, and the ability to inspire a team to achieve ambitious sales targets.As a Market Unit Manager, you will be responsible for developing and executing sales strategies, building a strong sales pipeline, and ensuring that your team consistently exceeds their targets. You will lead by example, providing mentorship and coaching to develop your team’s sales skills and capabilities while fostering a competitive and results-driven culture.Key ResponsibilitiesSales Leadership: Lead and motivate the sales team to meet and exceed sales targets, driving both new customer acquisition and increasing revenue from existing accounts.Team Management: Manage, coach, and develop the sales team to maximize performance. Set clear sales objectives, provide ongoing feedback, and ensure that the team has the skills and support needed to succeed.Sales Strategy & Execution: Develop and execute a sales strategy that aligns with the company’s growth objectives. Continuously evaluate market opportunities and ensure the team is positioned to capitalize on them.New Business Development: Identify and pursue new business opportunities, leading your team to target potential customers and expand the company’s market presence.Customer Relationship Management: Build and maintain strong relationships with key customers and stakeholders, ensuring high levels of satisfaction and long-term business growth.Performance Monitoring: Track team performance against sales targets and KPIs, taking corrective actions when necessary to ensure goals are met or exceeded.Collaboration: Work closely with internal departments such as marketing, supply chain, and portfolio management to ensure a seamless customer experience and to drive product alignment with market needs.Forecasting & Reporting: Oversee sales forecasting and pipeline management, ensuring accurate and timely reporting on team performance, market conditions, and sales opportunities.Training & Development: Provide ongoing training, mentorship, and coaching to help team members improve their sales skills, product knowledge, and customer engagement capabilities.Job Requirements Sales Experience: Minimum of 7-15 years in sales leadership role within specialty food ingredients distribution industry, with a proven track record of driving sales growth and leading teams to consistently exceed targets. People Management: Strong experience in managing and developing sales teams, with the ability to inspire, motivate, and lead by example. Sales Strategy: Expertise in developing and implementing sales strategies that drive business growth, including experience in new customer acquisition and expanding share of wallet within existing accounts. Financial Acumen: Strong understanding of sales metrics and the ability to manage forecasts, pipelines, and budgets. Communication Skills: Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels and inspire your team to deliver results. Problem Solving: Ability to analyze market trends, identify obstacles, and find creative solutions to drive business success. Customer Focus: A customer-centric mindset with the ability to build lasting relationships and address customer needs proactively.Tools: Proficiency in MS Office and CRM software.Key Personal AttributesLeadership: Strong leadership presence with the ability to drive change, motivate teams, and lead by example.Results-Oriented: A relentless focus on achieving sales targets and business growth.Coach & Mentor: Passion for developing others, with a track record of nurturing sales talent and helping team members achieve their potential.Entrepreneurial Mindset: Proactive and self-driven, with a focus on maximizing business opportunities and achieving goals.Adaptable: Resilient and adaptable in a fast-paced, competitive environment.Please note that this is a general description of the role and its requirements. The duties and responsibilities within this role may change over the course of employment at the employer’s discretion. About CaldicAt Caldic, we’re a global leader in distribution solutions, serving diverse markets across the world. Joining us means becoming part of an entrepreneurial, collaborative, and innovation-driven team.We OfferGlobal opportunities with rapid career growth and developmentAn inclusive, diverse, and dynamic work environmentThe chance to shape the future of specialty ingredient solutions across industries like food, pharma, personal care, and moreWe operate in 43 countries around the world. Our innovative solutions are tailored to meet the needs of industries such as food, pharma, personal care, and industrial markets, ensuring sustainable success for all stakeholders. To learn more, visit: www.caldic.comReady to Join Us?If you're ready to take your career to the next level and join a global leader, apply now at Caldic!Equal Opportunity EmployerCaldic celebrates diversity and is committed to fostering an inclusive work environment. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, gender, or any other characteristic.Location:Mumbai, MH, IN, 400034City: MumbaiCountry/Region: IN

title: Senior Director Eng, Buyer & Community
description_excerpt: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. _full_description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.What You’ll Do — Key ResponsibilitiesDefine and be responsible for the technical vision and multi-year roadmap in alignment with the org strategy.Lead, mentor and scale engineering leaders (Directors / Senior Managers) and their teams across architecture, platform, and product engineering.Deliver high quality, reliable products— set priorities, remove blockers, and ensure engineering execution across multiple teams and geographies.Establish and operationalize engineering standards: architecture and system design reviews, SLOs/SLIs, incident management, CI/CD, testing, and observability.Own capacity planning, engineering productivity metrics, and engineering budget (headcount, contractors, vendor spend).Drive hiring, retention, talent development, succession planning and performance calibration for senior engineering roles.Partner closely with Product, Design, Data Science, Security and Business stakeholders to align priorities and deliver measurable business outcomes.Be accountable for technical risk management — security, compliance, vendor risk, technical debt and scalability.Represent the org externally for key hires, vendor negotiations and major cross-org initiatives.Develop an inclusive culture that encourages continuous learning, experimentation and data-driven decision making.What success looks like High availability and latency improvements against agreed SLOs for core systems.Predictable delivery of major milestones and product launches (on-time, within scope).Improved engineering productivity metrics (cycle time, deployment frequency, mean time to recover).Successful hiring and retention of senior technical talent; reduction in time-to-fill for critical roles.Clear architecture roadmap and measurable reduction in technical debt.Strong cross functional collaboration and stakeholder satisfaction. Required Qualifications12+ years of software engineering experience with 6+ years in people leadership (managing managers) at scale.Proven experience leading multiple engineering teams (50+ engineers) building distributed systems, high-traffic services or platform infrastructure.Strong technical background (system design, cloud architecture, data platforms, performance and security).Experience scaling engineering organizations in India (hiring, local talent market dynamics, campus/recruiting).Proven success in hiring, mentoring and developing senior leaders.Track record driving measurable improvements in reliability, delivery and engineering productivity.Excellent stakeholder management — able to influence product, business and senior leadership.Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience.Preferred QualificationsExperience in marketplace / e-commerce / payments or consumer internet companies.Experience with microservices, event-driven architectures, and large data platforms.MBA or equivalent business experience.Prior experience with regulatory/compliance requirements relevant to the product area.Additional DetailseBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

_full_description: Aerospike is the real-time database for mission-critical workloads, powering millions of transactions per second with sub-millisecond latency and dramatically lower total cost of ownership. Global innovators such as Adobe, Airtel, Barclays, Criteo, DBS Bank, Experian, Grab, HDFC Bank, PayPal, Sony Interactive Entertainment, The Trade Desk, and Wayfair rely on Aerospike for customer 360, fraud detection, real-time bidding, machine learning, and agentic AI.Our mission is to unleash the world’s real-time data with a database built for infinite scale, speed, and sustainability. If you're ready to shape the future of data infrastructure, join us.Hybrid: 2–3 days/week in officeRole SummaryWe are seeking an accomplished Senior Director of Engineering to lead and scale Aerospike’s India Engineering organization. You will own multiple product lines, translate strategy into execution, guide technical direction, and develop high-performing engineering leaders. This role represents India Engineering in global forums and ensures we deliver robust, scalable, and impactful products.Key ResponsibilitiesEngineering & DeliveryOwn delivery of major product areas/platforms, driving roadmap execution, quality, reliability, and predictable releases.Translate company strategy into actionable engineering plans, staffing models, and quarterly/annual execution.Improve engineering velocity, consistency, and technical rigor across teams.Technical StrategyProvide architectural direction and technical oversight for core product areas.Champion performance, scalability, security-by-design, and resiliency across systems.Lead modernization and long-term platform investments while managing technical debt.Guide design reviews, postmortems, and deep technical discussions without becoming a bottleneck.India Site LeadershipServe as the senior engineering leader for India and represent the site in global leadership forums.Build a culture of ownership, urgency, and customer impact while managing budgets, headcount, and vendor/tools strategy.Hire, grow, and retain top talent; Strong technical grounding in distributed systems, cloud computing, or related architectures. Communication & InfluenceExecutive-level communication skills (written, async, and verbal).Ability to influence and align stakeholders across continents and functions.Strong track record working with cross-geo teams across multiple regions.Bonus Skills (Good To Have)Experience with implementing databases, storage engines, or distributed systems.Ability to participate in and guide design and architecture reviews.Deep understanding of cloud-native architectures and DevOps practices.Expertise in performance and scalability engineering.Expertise in systems built for high availability, low latency, and massive throughput.

_full_description: Teamwork makes the stream work. Roku is changing how the world watches TVRoku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.  About the Team & Role The Roku Pay, Core Payments team has played a pivotal role in driving Roku’s rapid growth in online subscriptions, powered by a seamless in-streaming, on-TV purchase experience. Supporting a wide range of Roku teams, Core Payments enables transactions across multiple consumer touchpoints — including TV, web, mobile, and advertising. These teams employ diverse strategies to sell digital content, from Subscription Video on Demand (SVOD) and Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) to Roku-branded devices and shoppable ads directly on the device.Core Payments is now seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the creation of our Payment Machine Learning Platform — a high-impact initiative designed to optimize payment retries, routing, and authorization success rates for our subscription business. This platform will harness AI/ML to make real-time, data-driven payment decisions at scale, directly influencing revenue growth, reducing churn, and enhancing customer satisfaction. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of machine learning, payments infrastructure, and subscription growth, collaborating with engineering, data science, finance, and payment operations to deliver a platform that is both technically sophisticated and mission-critical. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, highly technical environment, possesses deep knowledge of payments and subscription economics, and can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. You will own the roadmap, drive flawless delivery, and continuously improve the platform through experimentation and data-driven insights. What You’ll Be Doing Define and Drive the Roadmap: Partner with engineering, ML, and business stakeholders to define a high-impact roadmap for the Payment ML Platform, aligned to Roku’s subscription growth goals.Translate Business Goals into Product Requirements/Feature Guides, Convert business KPIs (e.g., authorization rate uplift, churn reduction) into clear product requirements, success metrics, and technical specifications. Lead AI/ML Product Development: Collaborate with ML engineers and data scientists to design, train, and deploy models for retry prediction, payment routing, and fraud prevention.Champion Experimentation:  Design and oversee A/B and multivariate tests to validate hypotheses, measure incremental lift, and drive continuous improvement.Hands-On Execution: Roll up your sleeves to write PRDs, define acceptance criteria, review model performance dashboards, and troubleshoot issues with engineering.Stakeholder Management: Communicate roadmap progress, risks, and learnings to executives and cross-functional partners.Post-Launch Optimization: Monitor KPIs, identify opportunities for improvement, and prioritize enhancements to maximize impact.Ensure Compliance & Security: Work with legal and compliance teams to meet PCI-DSS, GDPR, and other regulatory requirements for payment data.  We’re Excited If You Have 8+ years in product management, ideally with experience in payments, fintech, subscription businesses, or ML-driven platforms. Proven track record of shipping complex products in partnership with engineering, data science, and operations teams. Strong understanding of payment processing flows, gateways, acquirer optimization, and retry logic. Experience defining and measuring KPIs, running large-scale A/B tests, and making data-driven decisions. Ability to articulate complex ML concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfort working in fast-paced, highly iterative environments. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field; MBA a plus.    Our Hybrid Work ApproachRoku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy. BenefitsRoku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter. AccommodationsRoku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com. The Roku CultureRoku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.

title: Sr Product Manager - Marketplace Item setup

Joining Target means promoting a culture of mutual care and respect and striving to make the most meaningful and positive impact. Becoming a Target team member means joining a community that values different voices and lifts each other up. Here, we believe your unique perspective isimportant, and you'll build relationships by being authentic and respec
_full_description: About us:As a Fortune 50 company with more than 400,000 team members worldwide, Target is an iconic brand and one of America's leading retailers. Joining Target means promoting a culture of mutual care and respect and striving to make the most meaningful and positive impact. Becoming a Target team member means joining a community that values different voices and lifts each other up. Here, we believe your unique perspective isimportant, and you'll build relationships by being authentic and respectful.Team overview:Right along with Target’s distinctive retail brand, we are constantly improving and innovating our digital shopping experience. Are you ready to excel in the fast-changing retail environment? Are you a customer service enthusiast who’s curious about the guest and can see the shopping journey through their eyes? Then you’ll have success on the Digital Strategy & Platform team, whose goal is to offer guests a seamless online shopping experience across our platforms—including Target.com and the store network’s fulfillment capabilities. Here, you’ll collaborate to create and implement the digital strategies that enrich the online experience through the right mix of personalization, simplicity and intuitiveness. Your ideas will support Target in complementing the beloved in-store shopping interaction with a convenient, reliable and instantly gratifying digital experience.Role overview:We are seeking a Sr Product Manager - T+ (Marketplace) Item Charter to define and execute the strategy for how Target+ engages, guides, and enables sellers at scale. You will lead the strategy, vision, and execution of our Seller Portal. You will shape the ecosystem that empowers sellers, expands guest choice, and delivers scalable growthIn this role, you will lead a charter that improves seller activation, compliance, performance, and adoption across the marketplace lifecycle. You will own the systems, signals, and experiences that shape seller behavior—driving measurable improvements in marketplace quality, operational efficiency, and seller success.This is a highly cross-functional, data-driven role requiring strong product judgment, systems thinking, and the ability to influence outcomes without authority. You will partner closely with engineering, design, operations, and analytics teams to deliver intelligent, scalable solutions that help sellers take the right actions at the right time.As a Sr Product Manager, you will take lead on - Owning seller lifecycle engagement, including activation, adoption, performance improvement, and re-engagement. Designing data-driven nudges, reminders, and interventions that influence seller behavior and improve compliance, quality, and productivity. Enabling sellers to easily understand what actions they need to take and why, reducing friction and support dependency. Driving measurable behavior change in seller success. Define KPIs for marketplace success Use data and experimentation (A/B testing, cohort analysis) to refine features and improve adoption. Drive AI/ML powered insightsAbout you: Four-year degree or equivalent experience.6+ years of hands-on Product Management experience or equivalent domain experienceProven success in building and scaling marketplaces particularly in building seller-facing platforms - Item setup / catalog / PIM systems; Seller onboarding and listing workflowsOperational tools, workflows, and error management systemsStrong collaboration across business, tech, and operations teamsExceptional leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.Ability to think strategically while driving operational excellence.Must-Have Experience:Core experience in marketplaces (3P sellers, merchant tools)Ownership of catalog/item setup or listing systemsExperience building or improving seller onboarding / ingestion pipelinesStrong background in workflow systems, ops tooling, and error handlingProven cross-functional leadership (business + engineering + ops)End-to-end product ownership (beyond feature-level scope)Useful Links:Life at Target- https://india.target.com/Benefits- https://india.target.com/life-at-target/workplace/benefitsCulture- https://india.target.com/life-at-target/diversity-and-inclusion

title: Senior Product Manager (Payments Platform)
description_excerpt: As a Senior Product Manager specializing in Payments, you will drive the vision, strategy, and execution of innovative payment solutions that enable seamless, secure, and scalable transactions for our customers. You will collaborate with cross-functional teamsincluding engineering, marketing, sales, finance, legal and go-to-marketto deliver global products that delight users, ensure regulatory compliance, and support business growth. This is a highly visible role requiring global payments expert
_full_description: As a Senior Product Manager specializing in Payments, you will drive the vision, strategy, and execution of innovative payment solutions that enable seamless, secure, and scalable transactions for our customers. You will collaborate with cross-functional teamsincluding engineering, marketing, sales, finance, legal and go-to-marketto deliver global products that delight users, ensure regulatory compliance, and support business growth. This is a highly visible role requiring global payments expertise, technical fluency, and a passion for customer-centric innovation and growth.In this role, you will:Own and articulate the product vision and roadmap for Cvent payment products, ensuring alignment with company goals, evolving market trends, and regulatory requirements.Identify opportunities to expand our payment capabilities across the Cvent ecosystem through new products and/or partnershipsLead the end-to-end product lifecycle: ideation, problem definition, opportunity sizing, prioritization, development, launch, and ongoing iteration.Represent the voice of the customer by gathering feedback and translating customer needs and insights into actionable product opportunities.Define and analyse key metrics to measure customer and product success, leveraging data-driven decision making to inform strategy and prioritisation.Collaborate closely with tech and non-tech stakeholders to deliver high-quality, global payment solutions on time.Stay up-to-date on industry and technology trends, regulatory changes, and emerging payments technologies to inform product direction.Leverage AI to identify and solve customer problems, enhance product capabilities and improve decision-making processes.Identify key opportunities to use AI to externalise event payment capabilities and support AI-initiated workflows.Evangelise the payments products internally and externally, representing Cvent at customer meetings, industry events, and online communities.Required QualificationsBachelors degree or equivalent practical experience; advanced degree (MBA, MS) is a plus.12-16 years of total experience with at least 8-10+ years of product management experience, with a proven track record of delivering successful payment products at scale.Deep understanding of the payments ecosystem, including roles involved, payment processors, global payment methods, networks, risk/fraud management, and regulatory compliance.Experience leading cross-functional teams in a fast-paced, agile environment.Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills; comfortable working with large data sets and analytics tools.Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including experience presenting to executive leadership.Technical background or experience working closely with engineering teams is highly desirable.Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities and deliver results in a matrixed environment.Passion for building products that solve real customer problems and drive measurable business impact.Preferred QualificationsExperience in SaaS, cloud, or large-scale B2B software environments.Familiarity with AI and emerging technologies in the payments space.Must have experience with payments platforms, global payments, digital wallets, and/or e-commerce platforms.

title: Principal Product Manager, API Platform
description_excerpt: Who we areDigiCert is a global leader in intelligent trust. We protect the digital world by ensuring the security, privacy, and authenticity of every interaction. Our AI-powered DigiCert ONE platform unifies PKI, DNS, and certificate lifecycle management, to secure infrastructure, software, devices, messages, AI content and agents. Learn why more than 100,000 organizations, including 90% of the Fortune 500, choose DigiCert to stop today’s threats and prepare for a quantum-safe future at www.digi
_full_description: Who we areDigiCert is a global leader in intelligent trust. We protect the digital world by ensuring the security, privacy, and authenticity of every interaction. Our AI-powered DigiCert ONE platform unifies PKI, DNS, and certificate lifecycle management, to secure infrastructure, software, devices, messages, AI content and agents. Learn why more than 100,000 organizations, including 90% of the Fortune 500, choose DigiCert to stop today’s threats and prepare for a quantum-safe future at www.digicert.com  Job summaryWe are seeking Principal Platform Product Manager to define and lead DigiCert's API platform as both an internal engineering standard and externally differentiated developer product. This is a dual access role: inbound, you are the Platform authority who sets API structure, governance, guardrails and developer experience standards that all DigiCert product teams build to. Outbound, you are the thought leader who shapes the developer portal, external API experience and MCP strategy that customers, partners and 3rd party developers depend on. The person in this role will simultaneously be the most demanding internal API standard setter and the most credible external advocate for what a best-in-class digital trust API platform can be. The Principal Platform Product Manager is responsible to understand market needs and trends, collaborate with product teams horizontally across the portfolio, manage the delivery and success of initiatives and supporting platform components in a data-driven manner, and drive priorities that maximize platform value to both internal and external customers. The Principal Platform Product Manager will lead strategic and tactical activities for large, broad, and highly complex initiatives that impact all portfolio solutions, not limited to Platform. This is a critical, highly visible role charged with the strategy and driving the execution of priority initiatives at all levels across the organization. Individuals in this role will also mentor lower-level contributors. This role has a seat on the Platform Product Management team.  What you will doThe Principal API Platform Product Manager serves as the primary owner of our API Platform initiative, with substantial contribution and stakes in several other Platform initiatives. In support of this mission, this role will conduct internal and external research: customer requirements, market requirements, competitive research, internal research and analysis to inform strategic direction and priorities. They will establish a backlog of portfolio requirements that express dependencies on the Platform and actively maintain a prioritized stack-ranking to inform their executable roadmap and regularly collaborate across the business to collect and socialize a comprehensive, accurate and detailed set of feature requirements for one or more major platform initiatives. As the thought leader for API Platform, this role is responsible for constructing both the internal and external visions and leading discussions with functional leadership across the organization to gain alignment.  Primary responsibilities and Duties include: Outbound Elements:Own the collective API vision for Platform, including strategy, planning, and execution - and share accountability for the outcome Serve as externally facing subject matter expert with customer, partner and developer audiences to gain deep understanding of their needs, represent strategy and roadmap, solicit participation in proof of concepts, beta releases, advisory counselsServe as thought leader for API requirements at DigiCert: meet with analysts to discuss market dynamics and competitive positioning, publish articles/white papers, post relevant content to engage with their external stakeholder communityWork closely with Finance to develop Pricing and Packaging strategies Work closely with Marketing to develop GTM strategy, messaging, positioning and supporting artifactsInbound Elements: Own and understand API-specific platform requirements such as developer experience, webhook frameworks, API governance, security/compliance controls, automated documentation, version/change management; serve as subject matter expert for API requirements across functions to drive collaboration and achieve alignment on objectives, requirements, dependencies, design, and implementationLeads the decomposition of strategic objectives into phased operational and tactical goals and measures that translates into platform implications (e.g. APIs, metering, lifecycle management, reporting, portal), and work with the teams in each of these areas establish requirements, align on priorities, and choreograph deliveryLead the discussion to set business priorities that feed into the planning and execution of API Platform in close collaboration with other product teams and functions to ensure alignment with the needs of the business.Responsible and accountable for the technical vision, strategy, planning and execution - and shares accountability for the outcomeWork across all platform teams (e.g. Access Control, API, Common Services, Customer Experience) on roadmap planning cycles, backlog discovery and prioritization, pre-development design and alignment, and operational execution Be responsible and accountable for API Platform acceptance and success criteria of every internal and external releaseLeads the buy/build assessment for platform components where appropriate, along with vendor assessment and proposal Owns the technical definition, prioritization and hygiene of product backlog(s) throughout the concept through delivery processOwns the execution and outcome by partnering closely with immediate platform core team (Engineering, Architecture & UX) as well solutions core teams to represent business objectives along with supportSkills/Competencies:Proven aptitude for cross-functional diplomacy leading complex initiatives with portfolio-wide implications and impact across all organizational functionsDemonstrated ability to lead the solutioning to deconstruct technical challenges to meet stakeholder requirements and platform objectivesMastery of balanced decision making and decision follow-through – including problem definition, alternative definition and selection, trade-off analysis, success criteria definition, and overall execution Able to earn credibility as the internally recognized specialist on complex platform technical and business matters. Uses judgment within broadly defined policies and practices and solves most problems without assistance Experience and demonstrated dedication managing and meeting the expectations and requirements of internal and external customers; seeking first-hand customer information and using it to inform feature definition and development; bringing a cohesive perspective of the customers to their work Proficient decision making and decision follow-through - including defining a problem, identifying different alternatives, selecting and implementing the best alternative, and evaluating results to success criteria Highly detail-oriented and able to manage many tasks for multiple requesters simultaneouslyAbility to foster credibility amongst peers, to find common ground and solve problems for the good of all, represent their own interests yet be fair to other groups, can solve problems with with a minimum of disruption/escalation What you will have10 + years of experience in a technical or product roleProven experience and depth of understanding of Enterprise API Governance and best practices Experience with / working understanding of commercial API Platforms and the problems they solve for customers, partners and developers A working understanding of Model Context Protocol, and how it is used in association with APIs by internal and external stakeholders Credential or degree in business or engineering/computer science  Nice to havePlatform Product Management Experience MBA or Advanced Technical degree/credentials  BenefitsGenerous time off policies Top shelf benefits Education, wellness and lifestyle support

title: Principal Product Manager
description_excerpt: About the Company:At Playlist, life's richest moments happen when people step away from screens to move, connect, explore, and play. We're building the definitive platform for intentional living, connecting people with inspiring experiences in fitness, wellness, and beyond. With popular brands like Mindbody and ClassPass, Playlist empowers businesses and individuals, making it effortless for aspirations to become actions. Join us in reshaping technology's role to foster meaningful, real-world co
_full_description: About the Company:At Playlist, life's richest moments happen when people step away from screens to move, connect, explore, and play. We're building the definitive platform for intentional living, connecting people with inspiring experiences in fitness, wellness, and beyond. With popular brands like Mindbody and ClassPass, Playlist empowers businesses and individuals, making it effortless for aspirations to become actions. Join us in reshaping technology's role to foster meaningful, real-world connections.The Role You’ll Play:At the intersection of technology and global wellness, you’ll lead our international payments expansion. This role is about more than integrations and regulations—it’s about creating seamless, compliant experiences that empower businesses to grow and thrive across borders. Define and lead our global payments expansion strategy, aligning PSP integrations, market priorities, and compliance milestones Launch local payment methods and regional PSPs across key markets, owning the process from evaluation to go-live Build expertise in complex regulatory frameworks (like PSD2, Verifactu, NF525, and e-Invoicing), ensuring we stay ahead of local requirements Partner with Engineering to shape the global payments stack, from multi-PSP orchestration to routing logic and risk controls Collaborate with Compliance, Finance, and GTM teams to streamline reconciliation, settlement, and cross-border payouts Set success metrics for payment acceptance, reliability, and cost efficiency across markets Represent Payments in global initiatives, championing international enablement across product and platform teams Stay plugged into global payments trends, using competitive insights to inform product direction and decision-making____________________________________________________________________________________Experience You Bring: 8–10+ years of product management experience, with deep focus in global or cross-border payments Proven track record of integrating PSPs and enabling local payment methods across multiple regions Strong technical fluency—you speak API, understand payment flows, and can collaborate closely with Engineering Deep knowledge of authorization, settlement, and reconciliation processes, plus a solid grasp of merchant/PSP dynamics Familiarity with compliance frameworks like PCI-DSS, PSD2, and regional invoicing mandates Strategic mindset with a sharp eye for data—you're comfortable defining metrics and making decisions based on insight Exceptional communication and collaboration skills across Product, Engineering, Compliance, and Finance teams Bonus: Experience with B2B SaaS or platform-based paymentsHave we piqued your curiosity?Sound like the role for you? We’d love to hear from you! Even if you’re not 100% sure about potential fit, we still encourage you to apply. We’re looking for the right person, not the perfect series of checkboxes.The Company is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We highly value diversity at our company and encourage people of all different backgrounds, experiences, abilities and perspectives to apply. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, or other protected characteristics.By entering your email and phone number and submitting your application, you consent to receive emails, calls and SMS about your application and other roles at The Company, including by auto-dialer. Message and data rates may apply. Opt-out or text STOP to cancel at any time. If you are a California resident or reside outside the United States then by submitting your application you confirm that you have read, understood, agree and - where applicable - grant your prior, free, informed and express consent for the processing of your personal information, including sensitive personal information, as described in our California Applicant Privacy Notice or International Applicant Privacy Notice (as applicable).Note: This description outlines key responsibilities but isn’t intended to cover every task or duty. Additional responsibilities may be assigned as needed to support the team and business goals.

title: Senior Product Manager
description_excerpt: Senior Product Manager – Platform Efficiency & COGSAbout The RoleAs a Senior Product Manager for Platform Efficiency, you will drive continual “cost to value” alignment by delivering the product features and platform enhancements that ensure durable efficiency of scale. You will sit at the intersection of Product, Engineering, FinOps, and Pricing - responsible for the architectural efficiency and gross margin health of our global SaaS analytics platform.Sumo Logic processes hundreds of terabytes
_full_description: Senior Product Manager – Platform Efficiency & COGSAbout The RoleAs a Senior Product Manager for Platform Efficiency, you will drive continual “cost to value” alignment by delivering the product features and platform enhancements that ensure durable efficiency of scale. You will sit at the intersection of Product, Engineering, FinOps, and Pricing - responsible for the architectural efficiency and gross margin health of our global SaaS analytics platform.Sumo Logic processes hundreds of terabytes of data daily. Your mission is to ensure that as we scale, our infrastructure costs stay lean and our platform remains a performant, competitive, and high-margin engine. You will own the strategy for optimizing data ingestion, storage, networking, and compute costs while simultaneously improving the platform’s performance and reliability.Key Responsibilities COGS Strategy & Roadmap: Own the end-to-end roadmap for platform efficiency. Identify, prioritize, and execute initiatives that reduce cost-to-serve without compromising customer experience or SLAs. Own product strategy to focus on customer margins across multiple Sumologic revenue products Infrastructure Optimization: Collaborate with Core Platform and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) teams to optimize resource allocation across Kubernetes, Kafka, and AWS services (S3, DynamoDB, EC2). Cost Observability: Partner with FinOps to ensure internal "Cost Observability" dashboards inform continual efficiency improvements. Use data to identify "hot spots" in our AWS/Cloud infrastructure and drive targeted architectural shifts.Product Improvement Lifecycle: Translate complex technical bottlenecks into actionable PRDs. Define "Definition of Done" for efficiency projects, focusing on metrics like Cost per GB Ingested and Cost of Query engine, CPU utilisation of engineering assemblies. Cross-Functional Leadership: Coordinate across Finance, UX, and Engineering stakeholders to deliver cost optimized solutions that meet the needs of customers as well as the financial goals of the business. Clearly articulate the financial impacts of technical debt and infrastructure modernization to executive stakeholders. Vendor & Cloud Management: Work with cloud providers and 3rd party vendors to optimize licensing, reserved instances, and usage-based billing models.What We’re Looking For Technical Depth: You have a solid understanding of cloud-native architectures, distributed systems, and microservices. Experience with AWS cost structures and FinOps principles is a major plus. Data-Driven Mindset: You are a power user of data analytics tools and have a strong hold on cost and numbers. You don’t just report data; you find the "why" behind the numbers to drive strategic pivots. SaaS Savvy: 5+ years of PM experience, ideally in a technical platform role within an Enterprise SaaS environment (Log Analytics, Observability, or Cybersecurity). Execution Focus: Proven track record of taking high-level efficiency goals and breaking them down into testable hypotheses and delivered features. Communication: Ability to explain the ROI of a "refactoring project" to a non-technical stakeholder just as clearly as you can discuss API rate-limiting with a Principal Engineer.Pricing Knowledge: Experience with pricing, units of measure, and cost modeling. Technical Toolkit (Nice to Have) Experience with Big Data technologies (Lucene, Parquet, Spark). Familiarity with billing and provisioning systems (e.g., Zuora). Understanding of OpenTelemetry (OTel) and its impact on data pipeline efficiency. Why Join Sumo Logic?You will be working on one of the largest scale data problems in the industry. Your work directly impacts the company’s bottom line and determines our ability to innovate by freeing up capital for new product investments.About UsSumo Logic, Inc. helps make the digital world secure, fast, and reliable by unifying critical security and operational data through its Intelligent Operations Platform. Built to address the increasing complexity of modern cybersecurity and cloud operations challenges, we empower digital teams to move from reaction to readiness—combining agentic AI-powered SIEM and log analytics into a single platform to detect, investigate, and resolve modern challenges. Customers around the world rely on Sumo Logic for trusted insights to protect against security threats, ensure reliability, and gain powerful insights into their digital environments. For more information, visit www.sumologic.com.Sumo Logic Privacy Policy. Employees will be responsible for complying with applicable federal privacy laws and regulations, as well as organizational policies related to data protection.
