title: Senior Product Manager (Integrations)
description_excerpt: Cvent is a leading meetings, events, and hospitality technology provider with more than 5,000+ employees and 24,000+ customers worldwide, including 60% of the Fortune 500. Founded in 1999, Cvent delivers a comprehensive event marketing and management platform for marketers and event professionals and offers software solutions to hotels, special event venues and destinations to help them grow their group/MICE and corporate travel business. Our technology brings millions of people together at even
_full_description: Cvent is a leading meetings, events, and hospitality technology provider with more than 5,000+ employees and 24,000+ customers worldwide, including 60% of the Fortune 500. Founded in 1999, Cvent delivers a comprehensive event marketing and management platform for marketers and event professionals and offers software solutions to hotels, special event venues and destinations to help them grow their group/MICE and corporate travel business. Our technology brings millions of people together at events around the world. In short, we’re transforming the meetings and events industry through innovative technology that powers the human connection. Cvent's strength lies in its people, fostering a culture where everyone is encouraged to think like entrepreneurs, taking risks and making decisions confidently. We value diverse perspectives and celebrate differences, working together with colleagues and clients to build strong connections.AI at Cvent: Leading the FutureAre you ready to shape the future of work at the intersection of human expertise and AI innovation? At Cvent, we’re committed to continuous learning and adaptation—AI isn’t just a tool for us, it’s part of our DNA. We’re looking for candidates who are eager to evolve alongside technology. If you love to experiment boldly, share your discoveries, and help define best practices for AI-augmented work, you’ll thrive here. Our team values professionals who thoughtfully integrate AI into their daily work, delivering exceptional results while relying on the human judgment and creativity that drive real innovation.Throughout our interview process, you’ll have the chance to demonstrate how you use AI to learn, iterate, and amplify your impact. If you’re excited to be part of a team that’s leading the way in AI-powered collaboration, we’d love to meet you.Disclaimer: Beware of Recruitment Scams – Legitimate Cvent recruiting communications will always come from an official ‘name@cvent.com’ email. We never request any payments or ask for sensitive personal or financial information via chat or social media platforms. For more information, please visit: https://www.cvent.com/en/notice-recruitment-fraudResponsibilitiesDevelop an in-depth understanding of the business, products, including its goals and challenges, as well as a comprehensive knowledge of the customers and users of the platform. This involves analyzing their needs, behaviors, and pain points to ensure the product aligns with their expectations and delivers meaningful value.Collaborate regularly with product managers on the future roadmap, including integration initiatives and the SFDC roadmap. Brainstorm new ideas and prioritize features to ensure the backlog remains healthy, well-organized, and aligned with business goals.Become a subject matter expert in your product domain, possessing in-depth knowledge and insights that make you the primary point of contact for internal teams seeking guidance and collaboration.Develop detailed product specifications that begin by clearly articulating the “why” - the core purpose and value behind the product, feature, or enhancements. These specifications should be thorough in addressing all necessary aspects yet written in a clear and concise manner to ensure they are easily understood by all stakeholders.Write comprehensive user stories with detailed acceptance criteria that address all functional and non-functional aspects of the product, including security, data compliance, and other essential requirements.Work closely with engineering, design (UX/UI), and other stakeholders to define product requirements and user stories.Lead end-to-end product development process, from concept and design to development, testing, launch and ensuring timely and high-quality delivery.Lead PI (Program Increment) planning and actively participate in key SCRUM ceremonies, including daily standups, backlog refinement, sprint planning, and product demos.Should possess a solid grasp of technology platforms, integrations, and system design to collaborate effectively with engineering partners.Leverage the power of analytics and data-driven intelligence to assess product performance, user behavior analysis, gather customer feedback, and insights from multiple channels, and make data-informed decisions which enables you to drive excellence through iteration, improvement, and refinement.Monitor and analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to gauge the effectiveness, adoption, and product outcome.Share and present regular updates on the product roadmap and progress to leaders and stakeholders. Clearly highlight any potential risks and outline a plan to address them, ensuring everyone is informed and aligned on the path forward.QualificationsMinimum 10-15 years of career experience with at least 5 years of product management (PO/PM) experience in technology products.Strong product sense with an ability to articulate problems and envision solutionStrong understanding of technology and software development processes, with the ability to communicate effectively with technical teams.Inquisitive mindset with excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.Experience defining, launching, and evolving APIs and integrations at scale; clear understanding of API fundamentals (e.g., REST/GraphQL, authentication/authorization, versioning, pagination, idempotency, error handling, and rate limiting).Advocate and embed an AI-first mindset, integrating AI capabilities where they create strategic advantage, improve efficiency, or unlock new customer value.Proven ability to work hands-on with engineering and architecture teams; comfortable reading API specs, discussing system design, and making tradeoffs across performance, scalability, and cost.Ability to manage competing and evolving priorities in a fast-paced environment.Ability to handle complex situations, conversations and navigate ambiguity.Exceptional interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence, partner and build relationships across all levels of the organization.Strong written and verbal communication skills with an ability to communicate directly and clearly.Demonstrated experience working on at least one integration project, from discovery through delivery and measurement.

title: Senior Product Manager
description_excerpt: Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal
_full_description: Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators. Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely! Overview:Join our Data Integration team and take ownership of the Connect CDC product line. You will translate market trends and customer insights into a clear product roadmap. You will work closely with engineering, design, and go-to-market teams to deliver high-quality solutions. As the subject matter expert on Connect CDC, you will guide product development and identify growth opportunities. You will ensure the product continues to meet the evolving demands of our 12,000 customers worldwide. This is an individual contributor role with significant impact and visibility.What you will do:Define and own the Connect CDC roadmap, prioritizing capabilities that address customer needs and drive competitive differentiation in data integration and Change Data Capture.Conduct ongoing research with customers, prospects, and analysts to surface unmet needs and inform product direction.Author clear business requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria; maintain a well-prioritized backlog in close collaboration with engineering.Partner with Engineering, UX, Support, and GTM teams throughout the product lifecycle to ensure on-time, high-quality delivery.Monitor the CDC and data integration landscape and develop a working understanding of technical concepts such as connectors, pipelines, replication, and cloud/hybrid deployment.Align product direction with internal stakeholders across Product, Sales, and Customer Success; serve as the go-to resource on Connect CDC capabilities and strategy.What we are looking for:Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. A background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field is helpful but not required.Experience: 5–7 years of product management experience building SaaS platforms, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.Demonstrated ability to design and deliver reusable platform components—such as entitlement management, usage metering, tenant administration, and API-driven services—that support cross-team development at scale.Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams.Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha! or similar roadmapping and backlog management platforms.Familiarity with data integration concepts such as CDC, ETL/ELT, connectors, or real-time data pipelines.Travel is required: No.Bonus points for: experience with hybrid cloud deployments (particularly AWS and MongoDB), hands-on familiarity with Kubernetes or API integration frameworks, exposure to CDC technologies, background in data quality or data governance, or familiarity with SQL, databases, or streaming platforms such as Kafka. The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Senior Product Manager

_full_description: Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators.Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely!Precisely is an AI-first organization. All employees are expected to demonstrate proficiency in applying AI tools to accelerate their work, improve output quality, and eliminate low-value tasks. Candidates should be comfortable using generative AI tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT) in their day-to-day workflows, able to evaluate AI-generated outputs critically, and open to continuously adopting new AI capabilities as they emerge.  Overview:Join our Product Management team and become a driving force behind the Master Data Management Product (MDM) group. In this pivotal role, you'll delve into market trends across our existing and target customer segments, transforming insights into actionable business requirements, functional use cases, and compelling product solutions involving AI. Collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, you'll emerge as the subject matter expert on MDM, guiding product development and supporting sales with your deep product knowledge and market insights. Your expertise will identify growth opportunities, inform strategy, and ensure our products continue to meet the evolving needs of our market.What you will do:Manage Product Requirements: Gather and prioritize product needs from customers, sales, support, and other teams. Create clear documentation (MRDs and PRDs), and work with Engineering to build the roadmaps.Develop Business Plans: Collaborate and contribute to create long-term business plans for selected market segments. Track performance and update stakeholders regularly.Build Business Cases: Support product decisions, use of AI, and partnerships with solid business models and justifications.Communicate Effectively: Share updates clearly through documents, emails, and presentations. Ensure stakeholders understand market trends and product direction.Provide Leadership: Be the voice of the customer and influence the direction of the product.Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Marketing, Engineering, Strategic Planning, and other product teams to make informed decisions and expand product influence.Track Success Metrics: Monitor and report on key performance indicators to ensure goals are being met.What we are looking for:Experience: 8+ years of product management experience building data products, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. Background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or related fields is helpful but not required.Product Management Expertise: Demonstrated ability to design and deliver solutions specifically in product information management or other data domains—such as material master, customer master, reference data, etc.Experience with a broad spectrum of data management practices, including data quality, data integration, data governance, and metadata management.Experience with AI: Demonstrated expertise in justifying and embedding AI in products.Hybrid Cloud Expertise: Experience with hybrid cloud deployments and SaaS solutions, particularly in AWS.Strategic & Execution Skills: Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams and a high-volume feature backlog.Conceptual Technical Proficiency: Comfortable partnering with engineering on high-level architectural decisions involving APIs, integrations, platform services, and hybrid deployment models.Tools & Methodology Experience: Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha!. Demonstrated ability to influence without authority and lead cross-functional initiatives across product lines.Enthusiasm to work in a fast-paced, demanding business environment while collaborating effectively with cross-functional groups, management, sales and customers.Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage high volumes of workExcellent analytical, interpersonal, managerial, and organizational skills The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Senior Product Manager
department: Marketing Services, Staffing and Recruiting, and Technology, Information and Media
description_excerpt: Role: Senior Product ManagerLocation: RemoteEmployment Type: Full-timeCompensation: Competitive salary commensurate with experience, qualifications, and location. Indicative range: $140,000 – $220,000 (USD equivalent), plus benefits.Role OverviewWe are hiring a Senior Product Manager to own a major product area. The role drives strategy, prioritisation, and execution across multiple engineering teams — making sure what we build moves the metrics that matter.Key Responsibilities- Own roadmap and
_full_description: Role: Senior Product ManagerLocation: RemoteEmployment Type: Full-timeCompensation: Competitive salary commensurate with experience, qualifications, and location. Indicative range: $140,000 – $220,000 (USD equivalent), plus benefits.Role OverviewWe are hiring a Senior Product Manager to own a major product area. The role drives strategy, prioritisation, and execution across multiple engineering teams — making sure what we build moves the metrics that matter.Key Responsibilities- Own roadmap and strategy for a major product area- Lead discovery, validation, and definition for new initiatives- Partner with engineering, design, and GTM teams from spec to launch- Define and own the success metrics for your area- Mentor junior PMs and contribute to product practice- Communicate vision and progress to leadership and the broader teamRequired Skills and Qualifications- 6+ years in product management shipping software at scale- Track record of owning meaningful product areas with measurable impact- Strong analytical skills and comfort with quantitative trade-offs- Excellent written and verbal communication, including PRD craft- Experience working with senior engineering and design partners- Bias for action and ability to drive ambiguity to outcomesWhat You'll Bring- Curiosity to dig into systems and turn findings into shipped improvements- Strong written communication and ability to explain technical decisions- A test-and-learn mindset; you ship fast, measure, and iterate- Comfort working asynchronously across time zonesWhat We Offer- Fully remote, flexible work hours- Performance-based bonus structure- Annual learning & development stipend- Health and wellness benefits (varies by location)- Opportunity to work on high-scale, real-world impact projectsEqual Opportunity StatementThis is an equal opportunity role. Applications are welcomed from all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, ethnicity, nationality, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, religion, disability, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. All hiring decisions are based solely on qualifications, skills, and demonstrated ability.

title: Senior Manager SEO – SaaS Product SEO / AEO ( 7+ years )
description_excerpt: Senior Manager SEO – SaaS Product SEO / AEO ( 7+ years )Experience: 7 + yearsLocation: Remote (Work from Home) Employment Type: Full-time No of Positions: 2–3 Notice Period: Immediate Joiners Preferred Industry: SaaS / AI Platforms / IT Services Educational Qualification: Bachelor degree in Engineering from Tier I/Master’s degree from Tier I Role Overview: Lead product SEO and AEO/GEO for a SaaS platform, partnering with Product Marketing on new product launches and brand messaging, and driving
_full_description: Senior Manager SEO – SaaS Product SEO / AEO ( 7+ years )Experience: 7 + yearsLocation: Remote (Work from Home) Employment Type: Full-time No of Positions: 2–3 Notice Period: Immediate Joiners Preferred Industry: SaaS / AI Platforms / IT Services Educational Qualification: Bachelor degree in Engineering from Tier I/Master’s degree from Tier I Role Overview: Lead product SEO and AEO/GEO for a SaaS platform, partnering with Product Marketing on new product launches and brand messaging, and driving measurable growth across Google SERPs and AI answer engines through scalable content, technical SEO, and entity-first optimization. Success Metrics (example targets): Define and report weekly on non-brand organic signups/MQLs, top-3 ranking gains, organic share-of-voice in priority clusters (including launch pages), AI-citation wins (AI Overviews/ChatGPT/Gemini/Perplexity), and brand lift signals such as branded search growth and direct traffic contribution.Role Summary & Requirements:You will own SEO growth for a SaaS product—improving existing rankings, expanding high-intent keyword coverage, and increasing visibility in AI answer engines via structured content, entities, and technical excellence.Must-have experience:Scaled already-ranking pages (e.g., top 10 → top 3) through on-page, internal linking, and intent/CTR improvementsBuilt and executed keyword cluster expansion around existing winners to grow qualified organic pipelineDelivered measurable visibility in AI answer engines (AI Overviews/ChatGPT/Gemini/Perplexity) using structured data + entity optimizationOperated in a product + engineering environment (shipping technical SEO fixes, templates, and scalable content systems)Core competencies:Intent mapping across SaaS funnels (product-led, solutions, comparisons) with clear page-to-keyword ownershipInternal linking and information architecture to strengthen topical clustersContent briefs, editorial QA, and experimentation (titles/FAQs/modules) to lift rankings and conversionsTechnical SEO (hands-on or strongly partnered with engineering):Crawl/index control, templates, and site architecture improvements that move rankingsJavaScript rendering and performance (Core Web Vitals) diagnostics and fixes with dev teamsSchema/structured data strategy (e.g., Product, FAQ, HowTo, Breadcrumb, Organization) to improve SERP + AI readabilityAEO / GEO (AI Search Optimization):Create citation-ready pages: clear entities, definitions, comparisons, and evidence blocks; consistent product terminology across the siteImplement/validate structured data and on-page modules that increase extraction (FAQs, steps, specs, pricing/plan tables where applicable)Measure AI visibility via tracked prompts/queries, citation mentions, and overlap with priority clusters—then iterate content and schemaInterview expectation: bring one example of an AEO/SEO experiment or case study (problem → approach → results → learnings)Brand & product launch:Partner with Product Marketing on new product launches: positioning, naming/terminology, launch pages, and message consistency across web propertiesBuild launch-ready content systems (landing page templates, comparison pages, FAQs, proof blocks) that support both brand trust and conversionNice-to-have: experience running or collaborating on paid acquisition (search/social) and using insights to improve organic + AI visibilityLeadership & operating model:Lead analysts and content partners with clear priorities, QA standards, and weekly reportingRun a monthly roadmap across content, technical SEO, and AEO/GEO initiatives with measurable outcomesCoach execution and ensure repeatable systems (briefs, templates, internal linking rules, dashboards)Responsibilities:Own the SEO + AEO/GEO roadmap for SaaS acquisition across Google and AI answer enginesImprove performance of existing ranking pages (top 10 → top 3 where strategic) and expand clusters to grow qualified pipelinePartner with engineering on technical SEO (crawl/index, templates, CWV, JS rendering, structured data)Build topical authority via digital PR/backlinks and partner content where it supports product growthSupport new product launches with SEO/AEO-ready messaging and launch pages; align with Product Marketing on brand narrative and terminologySet measurement: dashboards for rankings, non-brand traffic, conversions, and AI citations; communicate insights to stakeholdersManage team execution, quality, and delivery cadence in an AI-first search environmentQualifications:7+ years in SEO for SaaS or product-led businesses; experience owning outcomes (traffic, pipeline, signups)Demonstrated wins improving already-ranking pages and expanding keyword clusters with clear measurementHands-on with Google Search Console, GA4, and at least one major SEO suite (Ahrefs/SEMrush) plus crawler tools (e.g., Screaming Frog)Strong technical SEO fundamentals and ability to partner with developers to ship fixesPractical exposure to AEO/GEO with a repeatable approach to improving AI citations/mentionsNice-to-have experience with paid search / performance marketing collaboration (ads copy, landing page testing, incrementality) to accelerate launchesPeople leadership: hiring/coaching, prioritization, and stakeholder communication
