_full_description: About the RoleWe are hiring for a Principal Product Manager – Platform for a high-growth fintech organization. This role will lead the vision, strategy, and execution for core platform capabilities that power multiple product lines spanning payments, risk, data, and integrations.You will operate at the intersection of engineering, data, and business, building scalable, secure, and high-performance platform systems that enable rapid product innovation across the organization.Key ResponsibilitiesDefine and drive the platform product strategy and roadmap, aligned with long-term business and technology goalsOwn core platform layers such as APIs, developer platforms, data infrastructure, identity, payments orchestration, and risk systemsPartner deeply with engineering, architecture, and security teams to design scalable, reliable, and compliant systemsBuild reusable platform capabilities that accelerate time-to-market for downstream product teamsEstablish strong product thinking in platform teams balancing internal stakeholder needs with long-term scalabilityDrive platform adoption and usability across internal teams through clear documentation, tooling, and developer experience improvementsOwn key platform metrics: latency, uptime, reliability, cost efficiency, and adoptionEnsure compliance with regulatory and security standards relevant to fintech (e.g., KYC, AML, data privacy)Mentor and influence other PMs; act as a thought leader for platform product managementWhat We’re Looking For7–12 years of product management experience, with significant time in platform / infrastructure productsStrong understanding of distributed systems, APIs, microservices architecture, and cloud infrastructurePrior experience in fintech, payments, lending, or financial data platforms is strongly preferredProven ability to build platforms that serve multiple internal/external consumers at scaleDeep empathy for developer experience (DX) and internal usersStrong analytical thinking with the ability to define and track platform success metricsExperience working in high-growth, fast-paced environments with ambiguityExcellent stakeholder management and influence without authority

title: Principal Product Manager
description_excerpt: Sabre is looking for a Principal Product Manager – Dynamic Pricing who will be responsible for the product vision, investment, planning, and execution throughout its life cycle, including increasing the profitability of existing products and developing new ones, and ensuring the delivery of the best solutions and meeting revenue and customer satisfaction goals. You will be proficient in the product, understand its structure and architecture, its capabilities. You will capture market needs, custo
_full_description: Sabre is looking for a Principal Product Manager – Dynamic Pricing who will be responsible for the product vision, investment, planning, and execution throughout its life cycle, including increasing the profitability of existing products and developing new ones, and ensuring the delivery of the best solutions and meeting revenue and customer satisfaction goals. You will be proficient in the product, understand its structure and architecture, its capabilities. You will capture market needs, customer input, usage, data and translate those into features that meet customer and market requirements and support the company’s strategy and technical innovation goals. The role possesses a unique blend of business and technical skills, a big-picture vision, and the drive to make that vision a reality.Role And ResponsibilitiesDrive product vision & strategy, business requirements documentation and analysisEnsure alignment to technology strategy throughout product life cycleManage the product profit & loss (P&L)Manage product area’s investment decisions, scope, roadmap, prioritiesUnderstand/monitor product's competitive and market landscapeConduct/attend visits, customer touch-bases, focus groups, conferencesSupport sales engineering and commercial/delivery/care teamsBe the product's subject-matter expert (SME) inside scrum teamsWrite scrum team stories, acceptance criteriaCreate, refine, prioritize scrum team's backlog, and accept workAttend scrum ceremonies and provide guidance to scrum teamsAnalyze, triage and troubleshoot maintenance issuesConduct demos (internal or external, as needed)Provide Quarterly Product Demo’s to Delivery and Sales Engineering teams.Qualifications And Education Requirements8 years of experience working with Airline or Airline technology4 years of experience in market-facing roles (e.g. engaging with customers and prospects)Business and technology-oriented, with a strong product and functional backgroundAbundant curiosity to delve into unfamiliar areas and quickly become proficientAbility to present a compelling solution to a business need that is grounded in technologyOwnership mindset of individual and team objectives; with both tactical and strategic thinking to ensure measurable resultsAbility to work independently and resolve professional, technical or operational project challenges in a creative, methodical mannerComfortable working in a team-oriented, highly collaborative virtual environment that is geographically distributedAbility to successfully navigate in a multi-stakeholder environment where there are often competing priorities, goals and challenges across our diverse set of internal and external customers and partnersExcellent self-organization skills, with the ability to manage and prioritize several projects in parallelStrong business acumen and situational awarenessMust have excellent written and verbal English communications skills, and be a strong public speaker comfortable presenting or demonstrating technology in front of large groups at all levels of an organizationProficient in Microsoft Office use for presentations and written communicationStoryteller and story mapperAble to well define a product’s feature setBachelors/Master’s degree in Computer Science, Business Administration or related fieldsExperience with Full-Service Carriers, Low-Cost Carriers or Travel AgenciesKnowledge of emerging trends in travel retailing and e-commerce (such as AI/ML, dynamic offers, etc.)Tech-savvy, ability to engage with Engineering colleagues to drive technical solutionsExperience with software development process with Java, C++, SQL is a plus

title: Lead Product Manager - Identity
description_excerpt: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future
_full_description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.About The Team And The RoleIdentity is foundational to how users safely access and interact with eBay’s global marketplace. The Identity team owns the core systems and workflows that power account creation, authentication, account security, different account models and trust signals across millions of buyers and sellers worldwide. As eBay continues to evolve its platform and expand its ecosystem, identity is increasingly delivered as a platform capability (Identity-as-a-Service) that enables secure and seamless experiences across products, devices, and partners and evolves from single-user accounts to multi-user, role-based identity systems supporting families and teams.We are looking for a Lead Product Manager to help shape the next generation of identity capabilities at eBay. This role sits at the intersection of security, platform infrastructure, and user experience, driving innovations in authentication, identity signals, risk mitigation, and account protection. As identity technologies evolve, we are also exploring how AI can enhance identity verification, fraud detection, and user trust signals, making this an exciting opportunity to define the future of identity at scale. This role requires deep understanding of identity systems and modern authentication patterns, balancing seamless user access with strong security and risk controls.What You Will AccomplishDefine product direction and drive outcomes across the identity lifecycle, including registration, sign-in, authentication, account recovery, and identity security.Own and evolve product strategy for key identity initiatives that balance seamless user experience with strong security and risk controls.Build and scale identity platform capabilities that support multiple teams, products, and services across eBay.Partner closely with engineering, security, privacy, trust & safety, and risk teams to deliver secure and resilient identity systems.Drive clarity on product goals, sequencing, and trade-offs across multiple identity use cases.Improve user access and authentication experiences while strengthening protections against account abuse, fraud, and compromise.Use data, experimentation, and customer insights to identify friction points and prioritize improvements across the identity journey.Ensure identity experiences remain trusted, compliant, and scalable globally.What You Will Bring10+ years of product management experience, ideally in Identity, Authentication, Platform, Security, or Trust & Safety domains.Experience building or scaling products in areas such as registration, login/authentication, identity verification, account security, or risk prevention. Familiarity with Identity-as-a-Service (IDaaS) concepts and modern authentication architectures and systems supporting multiple user roles, delegated access, and account governance.Strong product judgment and ability to operate across complex, multi-stakeholder environments.Comfort owning horizontal platform capabilities used by multiple teams at eBay and its subsidiariesTechnical fluency and ability to collaborate effectively with senior engineering partners on system design and platform architecture.Experience operating in global, high-trust, or regulated environments.Clear communicator who can operate at both strategy and execution levels.Demonstrated leadership ability with strong ownership and ability to drive initiatives end-to-end.Interest in leveraging AI/ML to improve identity signals, authentication flows, or fraud detectionAdditional DetailseBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

title: Principal Gen AI Product Manager, EMAP

We're passionate about unlocking the power of data to transform lives and create opportunities for consumers, businesses and society. We're a member of the FTSE 30 and for more than 125 years we've helped economies and communities flourish – and we're not done.Discover the Unexpected - Our 22k amazing employees in 30+ countries believe the possibilities for you, a
_full_description: Company DescriptionReady to make a difference? Experian has evolved into a global tech company and specialized in data and analytics. We're passionate about unlocking the power of data to transform lives and create opportunities for consumers, businesses and society. We're a member of the FTSE 30 and for more than 125 years we've helped economies and communities flourish – and we're not done.Discover the Unexpected - Our 22k amazing employees in 30+ countries believe the possibilities for you, and the world, are growing. We're investing in the future, through new technologies, experienced people and new ideas so we can help create a better tomorrow. To do this we employ 'big-thinkers' that share our purpose #uniquelyexperianJob DescriptionThe Principal GenAI Product Manager EMAP will be responsible for shaping and executing Experian EMAP’s AI‑enhanced product strategy across the Ascend and Fraud portfolios.This is a specialist role in the EMAP Product Management team. The role focuses on embedding Generative AI capabilities into core products, reimagining customer propositions, accelerating innovation, and driving adoption across diverse EMAP markets.Operating horizontally across Product, Engineering, Data Science, Client Delivery, and Market Product teams, the role acts as a bridge between global GenAI strategy, regional priorities, and market execution.The Ideal Individual Will BringDisruptive mindsetDeep understanding of generative AI technologiesStrong product instinctsAbility to convert opportunities into scalable, commercially viable features aligned to Experian’s product operating modelWhat You’ll DoGenAI Product Strategy & DirectionOwn the regional GenAI product strategy and roadmap for EMAP across Ascend and Fraud, informed by:EMAP customer VOC and market needsRegional regulatory and risk considerationsGlobal GenAI platforms, priorities, and capabilitiesIdentify where Generative AI can materially improve customer outcomes, product differentiation, and operational efficiency.Translate emerging GenAI capabilities into clear product intent, priorities, and roadmaps.Product Discovery & ProductisationLead discovery of high‑impact GenAI opportunities across customer journeys and internal product workflows.Define use cases with clear value hypotheses, success measures, and risk considerations.Drive productisation of GenAI capabilities, moving from concept and proof points into scalable, production‑ready features embedded in core products.Ensure solutions are designed for repeatability, supportability, and regional scale, rather than bespoke delivery.Scaling & Adoption Across EMAPWork with markets and regional functions to scale GenAI use cases across EMAP, with primary focus on customer‑facing innovation and considering productivity enablement where it supports product outcomesDefine how GenAI capabilities will be adopted, measured, and evolved over time across markets.Track outcomes including adoption, productivity gains, customer impact, and product differentiation.Embedding GenAI into Core PlatformsPartner with Engineering and Data Science to embed GenAI capabilities into existing Ascend and Fraud products, rather than standalone tools.Ensure GenAI features are explainable and auditable by design; aligned to Experian’s product operating model and naturally integrated into customer and analyst workflowsBalance rapid innovation with long‑term platform integrity, trust, and regulatory complianceCross‑Functional & GTM CollaborationAct as a senior product partner across Engineering, Architecture, Data Science, Client Delivery, Market Product, and regional GTM teams.Collaborate with GTM to shape AI‑driven value propositions, pricing inputs, and adoption models, ensuring clear and responsible client messaging.Influence prioritisation and solution design through strong product judgement, evidence‑based trade‑offs, and clear articulation of customer and commercial value.Operate effectively in a matrixed, global environment without formal line authority.Governance, Risk & Responsible AIWork closely with regional and global AI technology teams to ensure GenAI governance, controls, and guardrails are embedded into product design and delivery.Ensure GenAI product capabilities align with:Product development and risk frameworksLegal, compliance, and regulatory requirements across EMAPResponsible AI principles (fairness, transparency, traceability)Client Co‑Creation & Ecosystem EngagementCo‑create GenAI capabilities with selected clients to validate value, usability, and adoption.Partner with global technology and AI platform teams to leverage external GenAI providers where appropriate, in line with Experian standards and governance.GenAI Thought Leadership & StorytellingAct as an internal GenAI product ambassador for EMAP.Share success stories, learnings, and impact narratives to support alignment, adoption, and momentum across product teams and marketsWhat Success Looks LikeGenAI capabilities embedded into EMAP products at scale, not isolated pilotsTangible improvements in customer decision quality, speed, and usabilityClear, aligned GenAI priorities across product teams and marketsInnovation delivered responsibly, maintaining Experian’s standards of trust and complianceQualificationsWhat You’ll BringSignificant experience as a Senior Product Manager / Principal PM in complex software, platform, or data‑driven environments.Strong grounding in product discovery, productisation, and outcome‑based prioritisation.Deep understanding of Generative AI concepts, patterns, and limitations.Ability to translate AI capabilities into practical, customer‑ready product features.Proven ability to lead through influence rather than hierarchy.Strong communication skills with senior stakeholders and technical teams.Experience operating in regulated or high‑trust environments is highly advantageous.Additional InformationOur uniqueness is that we celebrate yours. Experian's culture and people are important differentiators. We take our people agenda very seriously and focus on what matters; DEI, work/life balance, development, authenticity, engagement, collaboration, wellness, reward and recognition, volunteering... the list goes on. Experian's people first approach is award-winning; Great Place To Work™ in 24 countries, FORTUNE Best Companies to work and Glassdoor Best Places to Work (globally 4.4 Stars) to name a few. Check out Experian Life on social or our Careers Site to understand why.Experian is proud to be an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. Innovation is a critical part of Experian's DNA and practices, and our diverse workforce drives our success. Everyone can succeed at Experian and bring their whole self to work, irrespective of their gender, ethnicity, religion, colour, sexuality, physical ability or age. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know at the earliest opportunity.Experian Careers - Creating a better tomorrow togetherFind out what its like to work for Experian by clicking hereExperian Careers - Creating a better tomorrow togetherFind out what its like to work for Experian by clicking here

title: Senior Product Manager
department: Software Development, Technology, Information and Internet, and IT Services and IT Consulting
description_excerpt: Toast is a revolutionary company where "going against the grain" is in our DNA. We empower the restaurant community to thrive by pairing deep industry expertise with powerful cloud-based software and restaurant-grade hardware.As an Enterprise Senior Product Manager, you will lead teams dedicated to solving mission-critical technology challenges for Toast’s largest and most complex customers. You will drive the roadmap and delivery of strategic features, integrations, and platform offerings that
_full_description: Toast is a revolutionary company where "going against the grain" is in our DNA. We empower the restaurant community to thrive by pairing deep industry expertise with powerful cloud-based software and restaurant-grade hardware.As an Enterprise Senior Product Manager, you will lead teams dedicated to solving mission-critical technology challenges for Toast’s largest and most complex customers. You will drive the roadmap and delivery of strategic features, integrations, and platform offerings that unlock long-term business value.About this Roll:*Strategy & Roadmap: Own the long-term strategic roadmap to improve product-market fit for Enterprise and Mid-Market customers. Agile Execution: Actively manage backlogs through creation, prioritization, and grooming of user stories in a fast-paced environment. Tech Savvy: Experience defining technical requirements for APIs, webhooks, and data models to support complex integrationsOperational Excellence: Monitor process and system health metrics to ensure optimal quality and delivery velocity. Stakeholder Management: Update internal and external stakeholders on roadmap progress and collaborate with R&D Enablement to communicate product updates. Ability to speak the same language as client and enterprise development teams, CTOs and architects. Cross-Functional Leadership: Partner with GTM teams (Sales, Marketing, Solutions) and evangelize product capabilities across the broader R&D organization. Do you have the right ingredients?*Experience: 7+ years of senior-level Product Management experience, specifically within the Enterprise software lifecycle—from ideation to delivery. Collaboration: Proven track record of working directly with customers, external development teams, and business partners to ensure alignment. Soft Skills: Strong program management and communication skills with a bias toward action. Problem Solving: Ability to prioritize workloads, meet deadlines, and solve complex problems using analytical skills. Relationship Building: Experience driving cross-team initiatives and building relationships across diverse personalities. Special Sauce:Customer Mastery: Background in software development, account management or client development for Enterprise customers. Influence: Ability to lead through advocacy and influence without direct authority. Domain Expertise: Knowledge of the restaurant and hospitality industry a plus; familiarity with Enterprise security standards (SSO, OAuth) and large-scale data architectureAI at ToastAt Toast, one of our company values is that we're hungry to build and learn. We believe learning new AI tools empowers us to build for our customers faster, more independently, and with higher quality. We provide these tools across all disciplines, from Engineering and Product to Sales and Support, and are inspired by how our Toasters are already driving real value with them. The people who thrive here are those who embrace changes that let us build more for our customers; it’s a core part of our culture.Our Total Rewards PhilosophyWe strive to provide competitive compensation and benefits programs that help to attract, retain, and motivate the best and brightest people in our industry. Our total rewards package goes beyond great earnings potential and provides the means to a healthy lifestyle with the flexibility to meet Toasters’ changing needs. Learn more about our benefits at https://careers.toasttab.com/toast-benefits.How Toast Uses AI In Its Hiring ProcessThroughout the hiring process, our goal is to get to know you. We use AI tools to support our recruiters and interviewers with tasks like note-taking, summarization, and documentation of interviews to ensure they can be fully focused on your conversation. All hiring decisions are made by people. To learn more: https://careers.toasttab.com/ai-in-hiringDiversity, Equity, and Inclusion is Baked into our Recipe for SuccessAt Toast, our employees are our secret ingredient—when they thrive, we thrive. The restaurant industry is one of the most diverse, and we embrace that diversity with authenticity, inclusivity, respect, and humility. By embedding these principles into our culture and design, we create equitable opportunities for all and raise the bar in delivering exceptional experiences.We Thrive TogetherWe embrace a hybrid work model that fosters in-person collaboration while valuing individual needs. Our goal is to build a strong culture of connection as we work together to empower the restaurant community. To learn more about how we work globally and regionally, check out: https://careers.toasttab.com/locations-toast.Apply today!Toast is committed to creating an accessible and inclusive hiring process. As part of this commitment, we strive to provide reasonable accommodations for persons with disabilities to enable them to access the hiring process. If you need an accommodation to access the job application or interview process, please contact candidateaccommodations@toasttab.com.For roles in the United States, it is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

title: Senior Manager - Product Analytics (Individual Contributor)
description_excerpt: Organizations everywhere struggle under the crushing costs and complexities of “solutions” that promise to simplify their lives. To create a better experience for their customers and employees. To help them grow. Software is a choice that can make or break a business. Create better or worse experiences. _full_description: Organizations everywhere struggle under the crushing costs and complexities of “solutions” that promise to simplify their lives. To create a better experience for their customers and employees. To help them grow. Software is a choice that can make or break a business. Create better or worse experiences. Propel or throttle growth. Business software has become a blocker instead of ways to get work done.There’s another option. Freshworks With a fresh vision for how the world works.At Freshworks, we build uncomplicated service software that delivers exceptional customer and employee experiences. Our enterprise-grade solutions are powerful, yet easy to use, and quick to deliver results. Our people-first approach to AI eliminates friction, making employees more effective and organizations more productive. Over 72,000 companies, including Bridgestone, New Balance, Nucor, S&P Global, and Sony Music, trust Freshworks customer experience (CX) and employee experience (EX) software to fuel customer loyalty and service efficiency. And, over 4,500 Freshworks employees make this possible, all around the world.Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.Job DescriptionWe are looking for a Senior Manager – Product Analytics (Individual Contributor) to serve as the hands-on Product Analytics Lead for one of our Product Verticals, owning product analytics end-to-end and shaping product strategy through data. This is a senior IC role for an analytics leader who thrives in ambiguity, partners deeply with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Design, and influences product roadmap decisions with strong analytical rigor. You will work with complex product telemetry, customer behavior, and business data to generate actionable insights that improve customer value and business outcomes, in close collaboration with Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams. You will also mentor 2–4 product analysts, acting as a subject-matter expert and raising the bar for analytics quality, experimentation, and storytelling across the organization.The Impact You Will CreateShape Product Strategy: You will own end-to-end product analytics for a dedicated vertical, transforming complex product telemetry and customer behavior data into actionable insights that directly dictate the product roadmap.Define the North Star: You will establish, evolve, and own the core metrics and KPIs that align Product, Engineering, Marketing, and GTM teams around customer value and business outcomes.Champion an Experimentation Culture: You will lead rigorous A/B testing initiatives from hypothesis to executive interpretation, embedding a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across all product teams.Elevate the Analytics Standard: Acting as a subject-matter expert, you will mentor 2-4 product analysts and raise the organizational bar for data integrity, advanced statistical analysis, and compelling executive storytelling.Responsibilities Own end-to-end product analytics for a Product Vertical with high autonomy and ownership Partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, and Design leaders to influence product strategy and roadmap decisions Collaborate with Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams to support growth, adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives through data Translate ambiguous business and product questions across teams into structured analytical problem statements Define, evolve, and own north-star metrics and product KPIs aligned to customer and business outcomes Act as the product analytics owner for instrumentation and telemetry, driving event design, coverage, and data quality Work with Engineering, TPMs, and Data Engineering to ensure scalable, reliable, and trusted data foundations Lead A/B testing and experimentation initiatives from hypothesis to analysis and interpretation Apply statistical techniques (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) to inform decisions Perform deep-dive analyses on large, complex datasets to uncover root causes and high-impact opportunities Build and maintain analytics products such as dashboards, frameworks, and self-serve insights for product and business stakeholders Communicate insights through clear, compelling narratives and executive-ready presentations to senior leadership Mentor 2–4 junior product analysts, providing guidance on analysis design, experimentation, and storytelling Champion best practices in analytics, including data integrity, validation, documentation, and reproducibility Foster a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across product teamsQualificationsQualifications & SkillsCore Experience10+ years of total experience in analytics or data science, with 5–7+ years of hands-on product analytics experience.Deep domain expertise in B2B SaaS environments is highly preferred.Technical & Data StackData Querying & Scripting: Expert proficiency in SQL for managing large, complex datasets, paired with strong hands-on Python skills for exploratory, statistical, and predictive analysis.Data Visualization: Proven experience building and maintaining self-serve analytics products and dashboards using Power BI (or similar tools).AI/GenAI Leverage: Exposure to utilizing AI and GenAI tools to enhance and accelerate analytics workflows.Analytical Methodologies & Product ThinkingAdvanced Experimentation: Deep understanding of statistical methods (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) and A/B testing design.SaaS Product Metrics: Expertise in product telemetry, event instrumentation design, funnel analysis, and cohort-based lifecycle analysis.Product Sense: Strong business orientation with the ability to connect raw data insights directly to customer adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives.Leadership & CommunicationExecutive Storytelling: Excellent communication skills with a proven track record of translating ambiguous data into clear narratives and executive-ready presentations.Strategic Influence: Proven ability to operate independently in ambiguous environments and influence senior product, engineering, and business leadership.Mentorship: Experience guiding and mentoring junior analysts in analysis design, data validation, and storytelling best practices.Additional InformationAt Freshworks, we have fostered an environment that enables everyone to find their true potential, purpose, and passion, welcoming colleagues of all backgrounds, genders, sexual orientations, religions, and ethnicities. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and believe that diversity in the workplace creates a more vibrant, richer environment that boosts the goals of our employees, communities, and business. Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.

_full_description: Job Title - Senior Product Manager Job Location - Pune, MaharashtraMUST Have Skills - Exp in building internal tools, ML models, and complex systems (e.g., pricing models, recommendation/discord systems), Exp in tools like Claude Code within dev environments to build prototypes and run analysis.Role OverviewOur client is looking for a Product Manager to own and drive automation within the detection and mitigation systems. This role sits at the intersection of product strategy, data analysis, and machine learning operations, you'll be responsible for identifying opportunities to automate threat response, validating those opportunities through rigorous data analysis, and working closely with data scientists and engineers to bring automation solutions to production.This is a hands-on, technically-oriented PM role. You'll spend significant time in the data yourself—validating hypotheses, understanding detection performance, and ensuring that automation investments deliver measurable impact against evolving attack patterns.Key Responsibilities Product Strategy & RoadmapIdentify and prioritize automation opportunities based on attack trends, operational efficiency gains, and customer impact. Define success metrics and track performance of automation features post-launchBuild business cases grounded in quantitative evidence—expected detection improvements, false positive trade-offs, operational cost reductions Data Analysis & Opportunity ValidationConduct hands-on analysis of detection data, traffic patterns, and attack signatures to identify automation opportunities Work with telemetry and truth data to validate that proposed automation will perform as expected before engineering investmentCross-Functional CollaborationPartner with ML researchers & analysts to translate detection research into productized automation capabilities Work with engineering teams to scope, prioritize, and deliver automation features on scheduleCollaborate with SOC and customer-facing teams to understand operational pain points and incorporate feedback into product direction Stakeholder EngagementCommunicate automation strategy and progress to executives, customers, and internal stakeholdersGather customer feedback and market insights to inform product directionSupport sales and solutions teams with technical context on automation capabilitiesMust Have5+ years of product management experience in security, fraud prevention, or data-intensive B2B SaaS productsStrong data analysis capability you're comfortable writing SQL, analyzing large datasets, and drawing actionable conclusions from ambiguous dataExperience working with ML/data science teams: you understand how models are trained, validated, and deployed, and can translate research into product requirementsHands-On Technical Drive:You are able to leverage LLMs and tools like Claude Code for analysis, prototyping and understanding code, and take ownership of complex problems, driving solutions forward with resourcefulness and urgencyTechnical fluency: Able to engage deeply with engineers on system design, trade-offs, and feasibilityProven ability to define and ship products from concept through launch, with measurable business impactExcellent communication skills: Able to articulate complex technical concepts to diverse audiences and build alignment across stakeholdersNice to HaveExperience in bot detection, fraud prevention, or cybersecurity domainFamiliarity with detection systems, classification pipelines, or anomaly detectionProficiency with data tools beyond SQL (Python, notebooks, visualization tools)Experience with Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban) and tools like JIRA/ConfluenceAdvanced degree in a technical or business fieldThis is a hybrid role based in Pune, India, with an expectation of three days per week in office. Relocation assistance is provided for candidates outside of Pune.

title: Senior Staff Product Manager
description_excerpt: OverviewSenior Staff Product Manager – Virtual Expert PlatformJoin Intuit’s Virtual Expert Platform (VEP) team as a Senior Staff Product Manager and shape the future of platform innovation at scale. This is a high-impact role where you will drive the strategy, design, and delivery of platform-critical capabilities that connect millions of consumers, small businesses, and accountants to the right experts across all Intuit product lines.We are seeking an exceptional platform product manager who br
_full_description: OverviewSenior Staff Product Manager – Virtual Expert PlatformJoin Intuit’s Virtual Expert Platform (VEP) team as a Senior Staff Product Manager and shape the future of platform innovation at scale. This is a high-impact role where you will drive the strategy, design, and delivery of platform-critical capabilities that connect millions of consumers, small businesses, and accountants to the right experts across all Intuit product lines.We are seeking an exceptional platform product manager who brings deep experience in building and scaling mission-critical platforms. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with strong technical acumen, proven ability to drive cross-company adoption, and a track record of delivering enterprise-grade platforms that unlock customer and business value.ResponsibilitiesWhat You’ll DoOwn and define the platform vision and strategy: Set a bold, multi-year direction for capabilities in Virtual Expert Platform, ensuring it meets the needs of diverse customers and scales across Intuit’s ecosystem.Drive platform architecture and scale: Partner closely with engineering and architecture to design resilient, extensible, and scalable platform solutions that power core customer and expert experiences.Enable platform adoption and reusability: Deliver services and APIs that are broadly adopted across multiple product lines, accelerating innovation and ensuring consistency in customer experiences.Leverage AI and data at scale: Harness AI/ML and data-driven insights to deliver personalized, proactive, and intelligent experiences across the platform.Execute with impact: Translate vision into strategy and roadmaps, drive prioritization, and deliver measurable outcomes aligned with customer success and company growth.Build organizational alignment: Collaborate with senior executives, cross-functional teams, and business partners to ensure platform initiatives have adoption, visibility, and strategic alignment.Continuously optimize: Improve platform operating models, processes, and governance to maximize scalability, quality, and speed.QualificationsWhat We’re Looking ForProven platform expertise: 8+ years of product management experience with significant time spent on platform products—driving adoption across multiple product lines or business units.Track record of scaling platforms: Demonstrated success in designing and scaling mission-critical, high-availability platforms that power core business functions.Strong technical foundation: Ability to engage deeply with engineering on architecture, APIs, scalability, and integration. Experience with AI/ML-driven products is a strong plus.Customer-first mindset: Passion for understanding customer needs, with a history of translating insights into platform strategies that deliver real value.Business and execution leadership: Data-driven decision-making, excellent prioritization, and a record of delivering impactful results in complex environments.Influential leadership: Strong ability to collaborate, influence, and align executives, engineering, and cross-functional teams around a shared platform vision.Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field required; MBA or equivalent experience preferred.Intuit provides a competitive compensation package with a strong pay for performance rewards approach. This position may be eligible for a cash bonus, equity rewards and benefits, in accordance with our applicable plans and programs (see more about our compensation and benefits at Intuit®: Careers | Benefits). Pay offered is based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and work location. To drive ongoing fair pay for employees, Intuit conducts regular comparisons across categories of ethnicity and gender. The expected base pay range for this position is:

We are looking for passionate, smart, and collaborative people who have a desire to do something meaningful and impactful in their career.We are looking for a strong Senior Product Manager who combines strategic thinking, healthcare market expertise, data-driven decision-making, and hands-on execution leadership.This role owns both product direction and measurable business impact — including engagement, retention, and revenue contribution.Only candidates currently residing in Gurugram, or those willing to relocate, will be consideredWhat You Will DoStrategic Product LeadershipDefine and drive product strategy aligned with company growth objectives.Identify opportunities to increase member engagement, retention, and platform utilization.Analyze healthcare and digital health market trends to uncover growth and differentiation opportunities.Build and maintain a clear, outcome-driven roadmap that balances innovation, scalability, and revenue impact.Engagement & Revenue OwnershipDefine and track KPIs tied to engagement, activation, retention, clinical outcomes, and revenue contribution.Drive initiatives that improve member stickiness and long-term value.Partner with business stakeholders to ensure product decisions support monetization models and expansion opportunities.Continuously optimize product performance based on measurable ROI.Data-Driven Decision MakingUse analytics, experimentation, and market insights to validate hypotheses and prioritize investments.Lead structured discovery processes grounded in quantitative and qualitative data.Make informed trade-offs based on business impact and long-term value creation.Engineering Partnership & ExecutionLead planning, prioritization, and execution within a dedicated scrum team.Own backlog strategy, sprint goals, and release planning.Work closely with engineering, data science, and design to deliver AI/ML and GenAI-powered capabilities.Balance speed, technical scalability, and business value in execution decisions.Ensure solutions align with long-term platform and shared services strategy.AI & Platform InnovationDrive responsible integration of AI and Generative AI into scalable healthcare experiences.Translate advanced AI capabilities (personalization, predictive insights, conversational AI) into features that increase engagement and outcomes.Contribute to reusable platform capabilities across conditions and markets.Cross-Functional LeadershipAlign engineering, clinical, compliance, and business stakeholders around clear priorities and measurable outcomes.Communicate strategy, roadmap, and impact to senior leadership.Ensure compliance with regulatory frameworks (HIPAA, FDA, data privacy).Qualifications8–10 years of product management experience, ideally in digital health, healthcare SaaS, or regulated industries.Experience building and scaling mobile applications (iOS and/or Android), with strong understanding of mobile product metrics, user experience, and release cycles.Proven track record of driving measurable improvements in engagement, retention, and revenue.Strong experience shaping product strategy based on market analysis and performance data.Solid understanding of healthcare ecosystem dynamics and digital health trends.Demonstrated ability to lead scrum teams and own delivery end-to-end.Experience delivering AI/ML-driven capabilities in collaboration with engineering and data science.Strong analytical mindset with ownership of KPIs and business outcomes.Ability to confidently engage with business stakeholders while working closely with engineering.Excellent English communication skills (verbal and written).Self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, evolving environments.***DarioHealth promotes diversity of thought, culture and background, which connects the entire Dario team. We believe that every member on our team enriches our diversity by exposing us to a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the world, identify challenges, and to discover, design and deliver solutions. We are passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable working and learning environments for all people, and do not discriminate against any employee or job candidate. ***

_full_description: Senior Product Manager (Integrations) |100% Remote | EltropyWe are seeking a detail-oriented and driven Product Manager – Integrations to join our fast-growing global team. In this role, you will own the strategy and execution of Eltropy’s integrations platform, enabling partners and financial institutions to seamlessly connect with our ecosystem. You will play a critical role in building and scaling our developer platform and partner integration capabilities, enabling integrations with core banking systems, loan origination platforms, account opening solutions, CRMs, eSignature/eNotary providers, and digital banking platforms. This role will also help drive Eltropy’s AI for All vision, powering an ecosystem where partners can build intelligent solutions and AI agents through our growing agentic AI marketplace.This is a remote position based in India, with 10% travel to client sites in the U.S. as needed.What You OfferMaster’s degree (or foreign equivalent) in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Systems, or a closely related field.Minimum of 3 years of experience in Product Management, Software Engineering, or a technical product role.Experience working with APIs, integrations, or platform-based products.Proficiency in Agile/Scrum workflows and cross-functional collaboration.Strong analytical and communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical needs into clear product requirements.Experience owning end-to-end product lifecycles, from ideation and roadmap planning to release and adoption.Technical Skills (Required)Experience using Atlassian’s Confluence and JIRA suite of toolsExperience working with REST APIs and integration architecturesExperience using Agile development methodologiesExperience using data analysis and reporting toolsBasic understanding of web technologies (HTML, CSS, JSON, APIs)Key Assets (Highly Valued)Experience working with financial institutions, fintech platforms, or financial services softwareFamiliarity with core banking systems, loan origination platforms, digital banking platforms, or CRMsExperience building or managing developer platforms or API ecosystemsUnderstanding of how community banks and credit unions operateExperience supporting partner integrations or marketplace ecosystemsExposure to AI-powered platforms, automation tools, or agent-based AI systems

title: Senior AI Product Manager
description_excerpt: We are looking for an experienced AI Product Manager who can operate at the intersection of product, technology, client engagement, and execution. This is a high-ownership, enterprise-facing role for someone who is excited by the opportunity to shape cutting-edge AI applications, work directly with international clients, and translate complex business problems into scalable AI-enabled products.The AI Product Manager will lead the definition, shaping, and execution of enterprise AI products and f
_full_description: We are looking for an experienced AI Product Manager who can operate at the intersection of product, technology, client engagement, and execution. This is a high-ownership, enterprise-facing role for someone who is excited by the opportunity to shape cutting-edge AI applications, work directly with international clients, and translate complex business problems into scalable AI-enabled products.The AI Product Manager will lead the definition, shaping, and execution of enterprise AI products and features for global clients. This role requires a rare mix of product judgment, AI fluency, consulting maturity, and hands-on problem solving. You will work closely with clients, engineering teams, designers, and delivery leaders to identify opportunities, define product direction, manage expectations, and bring AI-first solutions to life.The ideal candidate understands how to apply GenAI and agentic patterns in enterprise contexts, is comfortable navigating ambiguity, and is hands-on enough to prototype ideas, validate concepts quickly, and sharpen requirements through action. This is not a purely roadmap or coordination role. We are looking for someone who can think strategically, engage credibly with clients, and get into the detail when needed.Key ResponsibilitiesOwn the product definition and execution of enterprise AI applications, workflows, and features from discovery through release and adoptionWork directly with clients and internal stakeholders to identify business problems, frame opportunities, and convert them into clear product requirements and delivery plansShape AI-first user experiences, including copilots, assistants, agentic workflows, decision-support tools, and human-in-the-loop enterprise applicationsTranslate ambiguous business needs into structured product artifacts such as problem statements, user journeys, PRDs, acceptance criteria, prioritization frameworks, and release scopePartner closely with engineering, UX, architecture, and delivery teams to ensure product intent is translated into practical, scalable solutionsDrive workshops, requirement discovery sessions, stakeholder reviews, demos, and alignment discussions with enterprise clients and senior stakeholdersBuild lightweight prototypes, proof-of-concepts, and product mock flows using modern AI tools, no-code/low-code methods, vibe coding, and basic design/prototyping techniques where usefulHelp define and refine AI use cases involving agents, orchestration, retrieval, workflow automation, and enterprise system integrationMaintain a sharp eye for detail across business requirements, product behavior, user experience, and delivery qualityManage client expectations proactively by balancing ambition, technical feasibility, timelines, and delivery realitiesSupport value realization and account growth by identifying follow-on product opportunities, expansion areas, and innovation pathways within client accountsContribute to a product and delivery culture that values speed, clarity, experimentation, accountability, and customer impactWhat Success Looks Like In This RoleEnterprise AI products and features are clearly defined, well-prioritized, and shipped with strong adoption potentialClients see you as a credible product partner who understands both business value and execution constraintsProduct requirements are crisp, actionable, and reduce ambiguity for delivery teamsAI use cases move beyond demos into usable workflows that solve real operational problemsTeams make faster, better decisions because product direction, scope, and trade-offs are well managedClient confidence improves through strong communication, attention to detail, and disciplined expectation managementYou help create momentum for account expansion by spotting practical product and AI opportunities that clients want to invest inRequirements7-14 years of experience in ProApplication formDesign the application form for this role.duct Management, Business Analysis, Consulting, Enterprise Software, Digital Products, or related technology rolesDemonstrated experience working on software products, platforms, or enterprise applications in client-facing environmentsGood understanding of AI/ML and Generative AI concepts, with practical exposure to AI-enabled product design and deliveryExposure to building or shaping agent-based / agentic workflows or AI-driven assistants/copilots in a product or solution contextStrong experience in requirements discovery, user story definition, prioritization, stakeholder management, and cross-functional executionAbility to engage confidently with enterprise clients, business users, engineering teams, and senior internal stakeholdersStrong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to simplify complexity and present with clarityHigh attention to detail and the ability to manage product quality and client expectations in parallelA hands-on mindset with the willingness to prototype, experiment, and validate ideas quickly rather than rely only on documentationComfort working in ambiguous, fast-evolving environments where AI capabilities, client needs, and product direction are still being shapedPreferred QualificationsPrior experience in a consulting or client advisory environment is highly desirableExposure to enterprise domains and business applications is preferredExperience supporting farming-led account expansion, identifying opportunities within existing accounts, or helping grow strategic client relationships is a strong plusBackground as a Software Engineer or strong technical foundation is preferredFamiliarity with enterprise workflow systems, platform integrations, business process automation, or complex multi-stakeholder applications is a plusExperience creating basic prototypes independently using AI coding tools, prompt-based development, wireframing, or lightweight design toolsStrong educational background from top-tier institutions is preferred, including IIMs, IITs, BIT Mesra, BITS Pilani, top NITs, or equivalent institutionsSkills And Capability AreasAI product thinking and practical GenAI applicationAgentic workflow understanding and AI feature conceptualizationEnterprise product management and requirements rigorClient-facing communication and consulting maturityHands-on prototyping, experimentation, and validationCross-functional collaboration with engineering, UX, and deliveryStrong execution discipline and attention to detailCommercial awareness and account growth mindsetWho Will Thrive HereYou are likely to thrive in this role if you are someone who enjoys building in fast-moving environments, likes solving messy enterprise problems, and can move comfortably between strategy and execution. You are structured but not rigid, hands-on but not narrow, and client-focused without losing product depth. You bring ownership, curiosity, and pragmatism. You know how to earn trust in a room, ask the right questions, and turn ambiguity into actionYou should also enjoy working at the frontier of enterprise AI, where the best solutions require not only product thinking and technical awareness, but also judgment, experimentation, and strong partnership with clients and delivery teams Why Join AccellorThis is an opportunity to play a meaningful role in shaping real enterprise AI products, not just internal experiments or slideware. At Accellor, you will work on cutting-edge AI applications for global clients, solve complex business problems, and build products that matter in real operating environments.You will gain exposure to international clients, cross-functional teams, and high-impact business contexts. You will have the opportunity to influence both product direction and delivery outcomes, while building deep experience in one of the most important technology shifts underway todayFor the right candidate, this role offers a strong platform for growth across AI product management, enterprise transformation, consulting, and global client leadershipLocation / Working ModelThis role is based in Hyderabad or Bangalore. Travel may be required based on client and project needs.Reporting StructureThis role reports to the Head of Global Delivery and will work closely with leadership across Product, Engineering, Design, Delivery, and Client Engagement.BenefitsExciting Projects: We focus on industries like High-Tech, communication, media, healthcare, retail and telecom. Our customer list is full of fantastic global brands and leaders who love what we build for them.Collaborative Environment: You Can expand your skills by collaborating with a diverse team of highly talented people in an open, laidback environment — or even abroad in one of our global centers.Work-Life Balance: Accellor prioritizes work-life balance, which is why we offer flexible work schedules, opportunities to work from home, and paid time off and holidays.Professional Development: Our dedicated Learning & Development team regularly organizes Communication skills training, Stress Management program, professional certifications, and technical and soft skill trainings.Excellent Benefits: We provide our employees with competitive salaries, family medical insurance, Personal Accident Insurance, Periodic health awareness program, extended maternity leave, annual performance bonuses, and referral bonus

_full_description: Job Requisition ID #26WD96615Position OverviewAutodesk is a global leader in design and make technology. From architecture, engineering, and construction to media and entertainment, to manufacturing, Autodesk empowers innovators everywhere to solve the big challenges. Our solutions give design and make teams the power to connect their data, workflows, and teams so they can achieve the unimaginable. Are you ready to shape the future of making?Autodesk is seeking a passionate and experienced Senior Product Manager to spearhead the development and implementation of our Voice of the Customer (VoC) program. This pivotal role will be responsible for establishing a comprehensive strategy for capturing, analysing, and disseminating customer feedback across the organization. A key focus will be on partnering closely with our Customer Success organization to integrate VoC insights into workflows, use in companywide decision making, ensuring a closed-loop feedback system that drives continuous improvement in our products, services, and overall customer experience.As the Product Manager for VoC & Customer Success Integration, you will be a champion for our customers, ensuring their voices are heard and acted upon. You will define the vision, strategy, and roadmap for our VoC program, working collaboratively with cross-functional teams including Product, Engineering, Customer Success, Support, Marketing, and Analytics. Your ability to translate customer insights into actionable strategies and drive their implementation will be critical to our success.ResponsibilitiesStrategy & Vision: Define and articulate the vision, strategy, and roadmap for Autodesk's Voice of the Customer program & systems, aligning it with overall business outcomes and customer experience goalsProgram Development & Implementation: Lead the end-to-end development and implementation of the VoC program, including identifying key customer touchpoints, selecting appropriate data collection methodologies (surveys, interviews, social listening, etc.), and establishing processes for data management and analysisStakeholder Collaboration: Partner closely with Customer Success leadership and teams to understand their needs and identify opportunities to integrate VoC insights into their workflows, including onboarding, adoption, support, and renewal processesFeedback Loop Design: Design and implement effective feedback loop mechanisms within Customer Success workflows to ensure customer feedback is systematically captured, analyzed, and acted uponInsights Generation & Communication: Develop and implement processes for analyzing VoC data to identify key trends, pain points, and opportunities for improvement. Communicate these insights effectively to relevant stakeholders across the organization through compelling reports, presentations, and dashboardsPrioritization & Action Planning: Collaborate with Product and Engineering teams to prioritize customer-driven improvements and translate feedback into actionable product requirements and development initiativesTooling & Technology: Evaluate and recommend appropriate VoC platforms, analytics tools, feedback acquisition, and integrations to support the program's objectives and ensure efficient data collection and analysisPerformance Monitoring & Optimization: Define key metrics to measure the effectiveness of the VoC program and its impact on customer satisfaction, retention, and advocacy. Continuously monitor performance and identify opportunities for optimization and improvementAdvocacy & Education: Champion the importance of the Voice of the Customer across the organization, fostering a customer-centric culture and educating teams on how to leverage VoC insights in their workStaying Current: Keep abreast of industry best practices and emerging trends in VoC methodologies, technologies, and customer experience managementMinimum QualificationsBachelor's degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Computer Science, or a related field. MBA or advanced degree is a plus10+ years of experience in product management, customer experience, market research, or a related field, with a strong focus on Voice of the Customer programsProven track record of successfully designing, implementing, and managing VoC programs in a SaaS or technology environmentDeep understanding of various VoC methodologies and tools, including survey design, qualitative research, social listening, and analytics platformsExperience working closely with Customer Success organizations and integrating customer feedback into their workflowsStrong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights and present findings effectivelyExcellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence and collaborate effectively across all levels of the organizationDemonstrated ability to drive cross-functional initiatives and manage complex projectsPassion for understanding customer needs and advocating for their perspectiveExperience with CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce) and Gainsight is a plusFamiliarity with Autodesk products and the design and make industries is a plusLearn MoreAbout AutodeskWelcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made.We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world.When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us!Salary transparencySalary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience and geographic location. In addition to base salaries, our compensation package may include annual cash bonuses, commissions for sales roles, stock grants, and a comprehensive benefits package.Diversity & BelongingWe take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity-and-belongingAre you an existing contractor or consultant with Autodesk? Please search for open jobs and apply internally (not on this external site).

_full_description: Senior Product Manager - Unified Telephony | 100% Remote WFH (India)About the role: We are seeking a Senior Product Manager - Unified Telephony to lead the evolution of our Office Phone + Contact Center (CCaaS) platform into a single, cloud-native communication system. This role is critical in building a next-generation Unified Telephony Platform that seamlessly connects Contact Center (CCaaS) and Office Phone systems into a single, intelligent, AI-powered communications layer.Role & Responsibilities:Product Strategy & Roadmap: Define and execute the vision for unified telephony across Office Phone and CCaaS, aligned with business goals and customer needsTelephony Platform Ownership: Lead development of core capabilities, including SIP-based calling, IP PBX, extension dialing, call transfers (warm/cold), voicemail, and presenceArchitecture & Execution: Partner with engineering on Telephony architecture, call routing, carrier integrations, and scalabilityUnified Routing Experience: Enable seamless call flows between contact center queues and direct office users (extension-based dialing)Customer & Market Insights: Work closely with customers, sales, and support to identify gaps and drive product improvementsGo-to-Market: Support positioning, packaging, and pricing against UCaaS/CCaaS competitorsUptime Focus: Define KPIs (call quality, latency, uptime) and continuously optimize product performanceQualificationsExperience: 6+ years of product management experience, with at least 5+ years focusing on UCaaS and CCaaS solutions in B2B SaaS or financial servicesIndustry Knowledge: Strong understanding of IP PBX, VoIP, SIP, PSTN, cloud communications, and cloud contact center technologies.Technical Acumen: Ability to work closely with engineering teams, understanding APIs, integrations, and cloud and PBX-based telecom architectures.Agility & Leadership: Proven experience in working with complex, scalable systems with Agile/Scrum methodologies and leading cross-functional teams.Customer-Centric Mindset: Experience in gathering customer insights and translating them into impactful product features.Analytical Skills: Strong ability to interpret data, track key metrics, and drive data-driven decisions.Excellent Communication: Ability to clearly articulate product vision, strategy, and technical requirements to diverse audiences.TechnicalExperience with SIP trunking, FreeSWITCH/Asterisk, or carrier integrationsKnowledge of call routing systems (skill-based routing, queues)Exposure to AI voice or conversational AI platformsFamiliarity with compliance standards for TelephonyExperience with AI-powered contact center solutions, omnichannel communication, workforce optimization (WFO), or workforce management (WFM) toolsWhy join us?You will own a mission-critical product line that combines Back-office collaboration (Office Phones) and IP-PBX modernization with Cloud Contact Center, Skill-based routing, and AI-driven voice automationYour GoalEliminate fragmented telephony stacksUnify front-office (agents) and back-office ( staff) communicationDeliver a single platform for all voice interactionsYou Will Operate At The Intersection OfTelephony Infrastructure (SIP, PBX, PSTN)Cloud Communications PlatformsReal-time routing systemsEnterprise SaaS product designAI Voice automationAbout Eltropy (www.eltropy.com)Eltropy is on a mission to disrupt the way people access financial services. Eltropy enables financial institutions to digitally engage in a secure and compliant way. Using our world-class digital communications platform, community financial institutions can improve operations, engagement and productivity. CFIs (Community Banks and Credit Unions) use Eltropy to communicate with consumers via Text, Video, Secure Chat, co-browsing, screen sharing and chatbot technology — all integrated in a single platform bolstered by AI, skill-based routing and other contact center capabilities.Eltropy ValuesCustomers are our North StarNo Fear - Tell the truthTeam of OwnersEltropy is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.

_full_description: About LearneoLearneo is a platform of builder-driven businesses, including Course Hero, CliffsNotes, LitCharts, Quillbot, Symbolab, and Scribbr, all united around a shared mission of supercharging productivity and learning for everyone. We attract and scale high growth businesses built and run by visionary entrepreneurs. Each team innovates independently but has a unique opportunity to collaborate, experiment, and grow together, and they are supported by centralized corporate operations functions, including HR, Finance and Legal. Role OverviewNow we’re building a full AI-powered creation platform that supports them from the first spark of an idea to a finished, shareable piece of work.The creation suite is a key pillar of this evolution, the space where users can transform their words into visually compelling assets: presentations, campaigns, PDFs, social posts, and more.We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to lead this effort and shape the future of how people create with QuillBot.ResponsibilitiesLead the evolution of the creation suite from MVP to a flagship QuillBot product—driving the roadmap, success metrics, and user experience.Own the end-to-end user journey for visual creation—from ideation to layout to export.Partner with engineers, designers, marketing, brand, SEO and researchers to turn text and ideas into adaptive, multimodal outputs.Define and validate workflows, templates, and design systems that make non-designers feel like creators.Collaborate closely with the Writing and Chat product teams to ensure seamless handoffs between idea, draft, and design.Use data, research, and product intuition to balance user delight, technical feasibility, and business impact.Lead a cross-functional pod toward measurable outcomes—traffic, engagement, retention, and satisfaction.Qualification 6+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years building/growing creation or design-centric products.A track record of taking consumer-facing tools from 0→1 or 1→N, ideally with products in the creative or productivity space.Experience in AI-assisted or automation-heavy workflows—text-to-image, auto-layout, or generative creation—is highly desirable.Deep understanding of UX for creative tools and empathy for non-technical users.Strong design sensibility—you understand layout, visual hierarchy, and what makes creation feel effortless.Data-driven mindset; comfort working with analytics to inform decisions and measure success.Excellent communication and collaboration skills across design, engineering, marketing, and leadership.What You Can Expect to Do in Your 180 DaysFirst 30 Days: Learn & AlignDeep-dive into QuillBot’s creation platform vision, user personas, and current MVP.Understand the existing creation suite experience, performance metrics, and user pain points.Conduct a competitive benchmarking.Build strong partnerships with Design, Engineering, and AI/UX Research leads.Align on near-term success metrics and team priorities.30-90 Days: Define & PrioritizeCraft a clear roadmap for post-MVP development—defining which workflows, templates, and features matter most.Lead early experiments in user experience, template expansion, and AI-assisted creation.Establish analytics and feedback loops for measuring product success.Partner with marketing and growth to position the creation suite within QuillBot’s ecosystem.90–180 Days—Execute & ScaleLaunch new workflows or features that significantly improve user creation speed and output quality.Define and validate the long-term strategy for multimodal, brand-aware creation.Drive engagement growth and user retention through data-backed iteration.Build the foundation for QuillBot’s primary visual creation surface.Why You’ll Love Working HereOwn one of the most impactful growth and experimentation surfaces at QuillBot.Shape how millions of users discover and first experience QuillBot.Work at the intersection of AI, growth, and product systems.Collaborate with a team that values speed, learning, and thoughtful product decisions.Be a key driver of QuillBot’s transition from individual tools to a cohesive creation platform.Benefits & PerksCompetitive salary and annual bonusMedical coverageLife and accidental insuranceVacation & leaves of absence (menstrual, flexible, special, and more!)Developmental opportunities through education & developmental reimbursements & professional workshopsMaternity & parental leaveHybrid & remote model with flexible working hoursOn-site & remote company events throughout the yearTech & WFH stipends & new hire allowancesEmployee referral programPremium access to QuillBot*Benefits and benefit amounts differ by region. A comprehensive list applicable to your region will be provided in your interview process. Research shows that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't meet all the criteria. We strongly encourage you to apply if you're interested: we'd love to learn how you can amplify our team with your unique experience!This role is eligible for hire in India.We are a virtual-first company and have employees dispersed throughout the United States, Canada, India, Israel, Germany, and the Netherlands. We have a market-based pay structure that varies by location. The base pay for this position is dependent on multiple factors, including candidate experience and expertise, and may vary from the amounts listed. You may also be eligible to participate in our bonus program and may be offered benefits and other types of compensation.#Learneo Equal ​Employment Opportunity Statement ​(EEO)We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity and ​inclusion within our company.  ​We will consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender ​identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, ​veteran status, or ​ability status. We will ensure that individuals who are differently abled are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment as provided to other applicants or employees.
